full_name: Flowcrete Flowcoat SF150
description: Flowcoat SF150 is a solvent-free, two-component, high-build epoxy coating system that provides a hard-wearing, chemical and abrasion resistant floor finish on concrete substrates. It is used as a pigmented protective coating in plant rooms, warehouses, store rooms, and light production/process areas, and can be finished with an anti-slip texture. Key benefits include high chemical resistance, excellent abrasion resistance, and a seamless, hygienic, low-maintenance finish.
sow_steps: Surface preparation: light/medium shot blasting to achieve CSP 3-4 surface profile (grinding not recommended); remove all cementitious laitance to expose sound, dry, dust-free substrate.
Repair: fill damaged areas/voids/pinholes with Flowtex F1 mortar or Deckshield Putty; smooth rough areas with Flowcoat SC epoxy scratch coat.
Primer (if required): apply primer per Flowcrete technical recommendation and allow to become tack-free; substrate must be below 75% RH per BS8204.

Base coat: mix Base A and Hardener B with slow-speed drill and helical spinner for 90 seconds; apply Flowcoat SF150 base coat at ~0.30 kg/m² using a soft rubber squeegee, finish with medium pile roller.
Optional broadcast: fully blind with 0.4-0.8mm Deckshield Quartz aggregate at ~2.00 kg/m² approx. 10-15 minutes after base coat application; remove excess aggregate after 12-15 hours cure.
Top coat: apply Flowcoat SF150 top coat within 24 hours at ~0.40 kg/m².
UV coat (if exposed to sunlight): apply Deckshield UV at ~0.12 kg/m² for UV-exposed areas.
Curing/trafficking: allow foot traffic after 24 hours; vehicular traffic after 72 hours; full chemical cure achieved in 6-12 days depending on temperature.

full_name: Flowcrete Deckshield ID
description: Deckshield ID is a flexible, solvent-free polyurethane car park deck coating system that provides a colourful, crack-bridging, waterproof wearing surface for intermediate decks of multi-storey and basement car parks. It cosmetically enhances and brightens car parks while reducing tyre-squeal noise, and is suitable for both refurbishment and new-build projects. Benefits include high abrasion/impact resistance, slip resistance, and chemical resistance to fuel, oils, hydraulic fluid and battery acid.
sow_steps: Surface preparation: shot blast concrete/screed substrate to remove laitance and contamination; substrate min 25 N/mm2, dry to 75% RH per BS8204.
Repair: fill cracks/joints and form anchor grooves (min 3mm x 3mm) where the system terminates at movement joints.
Primer/binder coat: apply Deckshield SF (3-component, solvent-free PU primer/binder) at approx. 0.40 kg/m².
Membrane layer: apply Deckshield SF main/ED membrane layer at approx. 0.50 kg/m² for waterproofing and crack-bridging.
Broadcast: fully blind with graded Deckshield Quartz aggregate (0.1-0.8mm) at 2.00 kg/m² for slip resistance and texture.
Finish coat: apply Deckshield Finish (2-component coloured flexible PU coating) at approx. 0.55 kg/m².
UV top coat: apply Deckshield UV coat at approx. 0.12 kg/m² on areas exposed to sunlight for colour/gloss retention.
Curing: light traffic after 10-30 hours, full traffic after 16-48 hours, full chemical cure in 5-12 days depending on temperature.

full_name: Flowcrete Deckshield ES (with ED waterproofing membrane)
description: Deckshield ES is Flowcrete's polyurethane car park deck coating system incorporating a dedicated elastomeric waterproofing membrane layer, designed to provide crack-bridging waterproof protection for exposed and trafficked decks. It combines a PU primer/binder, an ED (elastomeric deck) membrane, aggregate broadcast, and a UV-stable topcoat, suitable for exposed top decks and ramps subject to weathering. It is engineered for heavy vehicular traffic while remaining fully waterproof and chemical resistant.
application_areas: exposed top decks of multi-storey car parks
sow_steps: Surface preparation: shot blast to achieve sound, contamination-free substrate with adequate profile; repair defects with epoxy mortar.
Prime surface with Deckshield SF (binding primer) for interior/exterior application at ~0.40 kg/m².
Apply Deckshield ED Membrane (elastomeric waterproofing membrane) at ~0.50 kg/m² to achieve crack-bridging and waterproofing performance.
Apply Deckshield SF intermediate coat at ~0.50 kg/m² over the membrane.
Fully blind with 0.4-1.0mm Deckshield Quartz aggregate at ~2.00 kg/m² for durable anti-slip texture.
Apply Deckshield Finish top coat at ~0.55 kg/m² for colour and abrasion resistance.
Apply Deckshield UV coat at ~0.12 kg/m² on all areas exposed to sunlight/weathering.
Allow full cure per manufacturer's schedule before opening to vehicular traffic.

full_name: Flowcrete Flowfresh RT
description: Flowfresh RT is a heavy-duty, HACCP International certified, antimicrobial polyurethane resin screed applied at 6-9mm thickness, providing a highly durable, lightly textured, hygienic finish. It is designed for heavy-duty wet processing areas in food and beverage manufacturing facilities, offering extreme temperature resistance (-45°C to 120°C) and resistance to a majority of acids used in manufacturing. The antimicrobial additive inhibits bacterial growth, making it ideal for hygiene-critical environments.
sow_steps: Surface preparation: shot blast concrete/screed substrate (min 25 N/mm2) to remove laitance, dust and contamination; ensure surface dry and free from rising damp.
Repair and level any surface defects prior to screed application.
Primer: apply Flowfresh primer coat to seal the substrate and promote adhesion, working it well into the surface.
Scratch coat (optional): apply an epoxy/PU scratch coat to fill surface irregularities and non-moving cracks.
Body/screed coat: trowel-apply Flowfresh RT (A+B mixed with filler C) to a thickness of 6-9mm using a notched trowel or pin screed, then spike-roll to release entrapped air.
Sealer coat: apply a PU sealer coat over the cured screed for the lightly textured matte hygienic finish.
Curing: allow light traffic after 12-36 hours, full traffic after 24-72 hours, and full chemical cure within 6-10 days depending on temperature.

full_name: Sika Sikafloor-264 (SG variant used in GCC/hot climates)
description: Sikafloor-264 is a two-part, high-build, solvent-free, pigmented epoxy resin coating used as a roller coat or self-smoothing slurry with good chemical and mechanical resistance. In the GCC, the Sikafloor-264 SG variant is specially formulated for hot and tropical climatic conditions and is certified as a low-emitting material under Dubai's Al Sa'fat Green Building system. It is widely used in manufacturing facilities, car park decks, workshops and as a broadcast system base coat.
application_areas: food and beverage wet process areas
sow_steps: Substrate preparation: ensure concrete is sound with minimum compressive strength 25 N/mm2 and pull-off strength 1.5 N/mm2; remove laitance, dust, oils and contaminants by mechanical preparation.
Moisture check: confirm substrate moisture content, relative humidity and dew point; if moisture content exceeds 4% pbw, apply Sikafloor EpoCem as a temporary moisture barrier system.
Priming: apply Sikafloor-161 (or -151/-160) primer by squeegee/roller/brush, ensuring a continuous pore-free coat; apply a second priming coat if needed.
Levelling (if required): use Sikafloor-161 levelling mortar or Sikafloor PS for minor surface levelling, or Sikafloor-190 Screed for higher-thickness levelling.
Base/roller coating: apply 2 coats of Sikafloor-264 SG by short- to medium-piled roller at 0.25-0.35 kg/m² per coat, crosswise application.
Broadcast system (for car parks): apply base coat of Sikafloor-264 SG, broadcast with Sikadur-507/509 aggregate, then apply sealer coat of Sikafloor-264 SG.
Curing: foot traffic after ~12-24 hours, light traffic after 2-4 days, full cure after 7-10 days depending on temperature (10-30°C range).

full_name: Sika Sikafloor-400 N Elastic
description: Sikafloor-400 N Elastic is a one-component, highly elastic, UV-resistant, moisture-curing polyurethane resin coating suitable for roller or airless spray application. It is used as a flexible, coloured waterproof coating on concrete decks and balconies where crack-bridging and weather resistance are required. Its single-component moisture-cure chemistry simplifies site application compared to two-part systems.
sow_steps: Surface preparation: ensure substrate is clean, dry, and free of contaminants (dirt, oil, grease, old coatings); pull-off strength should not be less than 1.5 N/mm2.
Priming: dilute Sikafloor-400 N Elastic by 10% with Thinner C and apply as primer by brush, roller or squeegee.
Mixing: stir Sikafloor-400 N Elastic mechanically for 2-3 minutes at low speed (300-450 rpm); add Thinner C or Extender T if required.
Coating application: pour and spread the coating evenly with a trowel, brush, roller (solvent-resistant) or airless spray in multiple layers.
Recoat window: apply subsequent coats within 3 to 72 hours at 20°C depending on site conditions.
Curing: protect from rain/moisture during initial cure; substrate temperature must be maintained between +10°C and +30°C during application.

full_name: Sika Sikafloor-21 PurCem with Sikalastic waterproofing membrane
description: The Sikafloor PurCem system combines Sika's polyurethane-cement hybrid screed technology with an integrated waterproofing membrane layer for use where both structural waterproofing and heavy-duty chemical/mechanical resistance are needed. Sikafloor-21 PurCem (AE) is a multi-component, water-based, coloured PU hybrid screed with self-smoothing properties, suitable for hot/tropical climates and certified as low-emitting under Dubai's Al Sa'fat system. It can be applied over young/damp concrete and combined with waterproofing membranes for podium decks and wet trafficked areas.
application_areas: car park ramps with waterproofing requirement
sow_steps: Substrate preparation: ensure concrete/screed is sound, clean and profiled by shot blasting; substrate can be damp/young concrete (even 7 days old).
Waterproofing membrane: apply Sika waterproofing membrane layer (e.g., Sikalastic or Sikafloor primer-membrane system) where structural waterproofing is required beneath the screed.
Scratch coat: mix and trowel-apply Sikafloor-21 PurCem scratch coat at approx. 1.5mm thickness (~2.9 kg/m²) to seal the surface and fill irregularities; allow 16-24 hours cure at +20°C.
Body/wear coat: pour Sikafloor-21 PurCem body coat and spread with toothed trowel or pin screed to 3-6mm thickness; remove entrapped air with a spike roller.
Finish: use steel trowel finish for smooth matt appearance or broadcast aggregate for slip resistance.
Curing: allow 24-72 hours before overcoating depending on ambient temperature; protect from water/condensation for at least 24 hours.

full_name: Sika Sikafloor-21 PurCem (AE)
description: Sikafloor-21 PurCem (AE) is a multi-component, water-based, coloured polyurethane hybrid screed with self-smoothing properties, typically installed at 3-6mm thickness with a smooth, matt, impervious, hard-wearing surface. Formulated for hot and tropical climates, it offers very good chemical resistance, high mechanical resistance and can be applied to substrates with high moisture content (even 7-day old damp concrete). It is used as a scratch coat, base coat and wear coat in Sikafloor PurCem system build-ups for medium to heavy loading and high chemical exposure areas.
sow_steps: Substrate preparation: shot blast/prepare concrete to a sound, clean, contaminant-free surface with adequate profile; substrate up to 90% RH acceptable.
Scratch coat: apply Sikafloor-21 PurCem scratch coat using steel trowels at ~1-2mm thickness to seal surface and fill minor defects; cure 16-24 hours at +20°C.
Priming (where required): allow scratch coat to act as bonding layer for the body coat.
Body and wear coat: mix parts A, B and C (aggregate) using a double paddle mixer; pour and spread with a toothed trowel or pin screed to 3-6mm thickness.
De-airing: spike-roll the surface to release entrapped air and achieve an even, flat finish.
Finishing: steel trowel or roller finish for smooth matt surface; ensure wet edges are joined promptly to avoid visible seams.
Curing: material should be conditioned at +15°C to +25°C for 48 hours before use; allow 24-72 hours before overcoating/trafficking depending on temperature.

full_name: Fosroc Nitoflor FC150
description: Nitoflor FC150 is a solvent-free, high-build epoxy resin floor coating that provides a hard-wearing, chemical and abrasion resistant floor finish, specially formulated for use in Middle East conditions. It is ideally suited for wet areas requiring high resistance to chemicals, oils and grease such as dairies, soft drink production facilities, chemical plants, car parks and workshops. Benefits include durability, low maintenance, and a wide range of colours with optional anti-slip texture.
sow_steps: Surface preparation: shot blast or mechanically prepare concrete substrate to remove laitance, dust and contamination, achieving a sound open-textured surface.
Priming: apply appropriate Fosroc epoxy primer to the prepared substrate, ensuring full pore-free coverage.
Mixing: combine pre-weighed base and hardener components with the Nitoflor colour pack using a slow-speed drill, avoiding air entrainment.
Base coat: apply Nitoflor FC150 base coat by roller/squeegee to achieve minimum 400 micron dry film thickness.
Anti-slip texture (optional): broadcast Nitoflor Antislip Grains into the wet base coat for slip-resistant finish.
Top coat: apply a sealing top coat of Nitoflor FC150 over the textured base coat.
Curing: allow full cure per manufacturer's schedule before subjecting to chemical or heavy traffic exposure.

full_name: Fosroc Dekguard PU100
description: Dekguard PU100 is a polyurethane-based protective floor coating from Fosroc's Dekguard range, designed for durable, weather-resistant surface protection on concrete decks and trafficked areas. It provides good chemical and abrasion resistance suited to Middle East climatic conditions. It is part of Fosroc's broader protective coatings portfolio used across car parks and industrial floor applications.
sow_steps: Surface preparation: shot blast or mechanically prepare the concrete substrate to remove laitance and contaminants, achieving a sound profile.
Repair: fill cracks, spalls and surface defects with appropriate Fosroc repair mortar.
Priming: apply a compatible Fosroc primer to promote adhesion, allowing it to become tack-free.
Coating application: apply Dekguard PU100 by roller or squeegee at the manufacturer's recommended coverage rate.
Broadcast (if anti-slip required): apply graded aggregate broadcast into the wet coating for texture.
Top coat: apply a final sealing/UV-resistant top coat where exposed to sunlight.
Curing: allow full cure per Fosroc's technical data sheet before trafficking.

full_name: Fosroc Nitoflor TF120 UB (with waterproofing membrane build-up)
description: Nitoflor TF120 UB is a heavy-duty, PU-based floor screed system resistant to impact, abrasion, thermal shock, steam and hot water, applied at 5-12mm thickness, often combined with an underlying waterproofing membrane layer for wet processing environments. It is part of Fosroc's Nitoflor PU-blended range designed for the most demanding food and beverage and electronic assembly areas. The system provides both structural waterproofing protection and a hygienic, chemically resistant wear surface.
sow_steps: Surface preparation: shot blast concrete substrate to remove laitance and achieve a sound, contaminant-free profile.
Waterproofing membrane (where required): apply Fosroc waterproofing membrane/primer system beneath the screed for areas needing sub-slab moisture protection.
Priming: apply a compatible epoxy or PU primer to seal the substrate and promote bond strength.
Screed application: trowel-apply the PU-blended Nitoflor TF120 UB screed to 5-12mm thickness using a notched trowel.
De-airing: spike-roll the freshly laid screed to remove entrapped air and achieve a uniform, dense surface.
Sealer coat: apply a sealer/topcoat for the final hygienic, wash-down resistant finish.
Curing: allow full cure per manufacturer's technical data sheet before exposure to steam cleaning or heavy traffic.

full_name: Fosroc Nitoflor SL3000 UT
description: Nitoflor SL3000 UT is a flow-applied, PU cementitious-based floor topping system installed at 4-6mm thickness, designed for heavy duty industrial environments needing chemical resistance combined with mechanical strength. It provides a dense, hygienic, impervious surface suitable for food, chemical, pharmaceutical and dry processing facilities. This cementitious-PU hybrid technology is well suited to Fosroc's Middle East product range for demanding process areas.
sow_steps: Surface preparation: shot blast concrete substrate to achieve a sound, dust-free, contaminant-free surface with adequate profile.
Repair: fill cracks and surface defects with an appropriate repair mortar prior to screed application.
Priming: apply Fosroc-recommended primer to saturate substrate porosity and enhance adhesion.
Screed pour: mix and pour the Nitoflor SL3000 UT PU-cementitious blend, spreading with a notched trowel or pin screed to 4-6mm thickness.
De-airing: spike-roll the poured screed to release entrapped air and level the surface.
Finishing: trowel or roller finish for the required smooth or lightly textured surface.
Curing: allow to cure per technical data sheet before subjecting to full traffic and chemical exposure.

full_name: Jotun Jotafloor EPC 300 Plus
description: Jotafloor EPC 300 Plus is a light- and medium-duty, solvent-free, self-smoothing epoxy floor coating engineered for improved longevity, strength and performance. It withstands up to 10 tons of dynamic load and is suitable for rubber tyre traffic, offering increased durability, water resistance and easy maintenance on well-prepared concrete surfaces. It is used across a wide range of spaces including industrial floors, car parks, workshops and warehouses.
sow_steps: Surface preparation: mechanically prepare (shot blast/grind) the concrete substrate to remove laitance and achieve a sound, clean, dry surface profile.
Repair: fill cracks and surface imperfections prior to coating.
Priming: apply an appropriate Jotun epoxy primer to the prepared concrete and allow to cure.
Mixing: combine two-component Jotafloor EPC 300 Plus (base and hardener) using a slow-speed mixer to a homogeneous consistency.
Self-smoothing application: pour and spread the mixed coating with a notched trowel or squeegee to the specified thickness, then back-roll or spike-roll to release entrapped air.
Curing: allow the coating to cure to touch-dry and then full cure per the technical data sheet before opening to traffic.
Maintenance: clean regularly with mild detergent to preserve surface finish.

full_name: Jotun Jotafloor PU Topcoat
description: Jotafloor PU Topcoat is a high-performance, light-duty, two-component isocyanate-cured polyurethane coating built to resist chemicals, abrasion, impact and sun damage. It is recommended for concrete floors in car parks, parking bays, pedestrian walkways, driveways, ramps and traffic deck systems, and is specially designed for gloss and colour retention in areas exposed to direct sunlight. It is tintable in a wide range of colours through Jotun's Multicolor Industry (MCI) tinting system.
sow_steps: Surface preparation: shot blast or mechanically prepare the concrete substrate to remove laitance and achieve a sound, dry, clean surface.
Repair: fill cracks and surface defects with appropriate repair mortar.
Priming: apply an approved Jotun primer to the prepared substrate and allow to cure/tack-free.
Base/mid coat (if system includes waterproof layer): apply Jotafloor PU Universal or similar mid-coat as a waterproof membrane where required.
Topcoat mixing: mix the two-component Jotafloor PU Topcoat thoroughly before application.
Topcoat application: apply evenly by roller or squeegee over the approved primer/mid-coat, adding Jotafloor Non-Slip Aggregate if enhanced slip resistance is needed.
Curing: allow the coating to cure fully per the technical data sheet before opening to vehicular or pedestrian traffic.

full_name: Jotun Jotafloor PU Universal (with waterproof membrane function)
description: Jotafloor PU Universal is a two-component, aromatic isocyanate-cured polyurethane coating suitable for creating seamless floors, notable for acting as a waterproof membrane when applied as a mid-coat. It is a high-impact resistant, solvent-free product with high compressive strength, abrasion resistance and good weathering resistance, making it excellent for heavy-duty traffic areas. It is recommended for car parks, ramps, industrial floors, roof decks, pedestrian walkways, laboratories, hospitals, food-manufacturing facilities and dairies.
sow_steps: Surface preparation: shot blast concrete substrate to remove laitance and achieve a sound, dry, clean surface with adequate profile.
Repair: fill cracks and defects with suitable repair mortar; form movement joints where required.
Priming: apply an approved epoxy or PU primer, allowing it to cure to tack-free before overcoating.
Mid-coat/membrane application: apply Jotafloor PU Universal as a waterproof membrane mid-coat by roller or squeegee at the specified coverage rate.
Broadcast (if required): incorporate aggregate broadcast for enhanced slip resistance where specified.
Topcoat: apply Jotafloor PU Topcoat or PU Ultra as the final UV-stable wear layer.
Curing: allow full cure per technical data sheet before subjecting to vehicular traffic or water exposure.

full_name: Jotun Jotafloor PU Ultra
description: Jotafloor PU Ultra is an ultra-premium, highly elastomeric, UV-resistant, isocyanate-cured aliphatic polyurethane coating with superior elongation and crack-bridging properties, complying with ASTM C957. It is a non-yellowing product with excellent gloss and colour retention, good chemical, abrasion and impact resistance, and is LEED compliant. It is recommended as a topcoat/wear layer for rooftops, car parks, parking bays, pedestrian walkways, industrial floors, driveways, ramps and traffic deck systems, especially where UV exposure and elongation performance are critical.
sow_steps: Surface preparation: shot blast or mechanically prepare the concrete substrate to achieve a sound, dry, contaminant-free surface.
Repair: address cracks and surface defects with appropriate repair mortar.
Priming: apply an approved Jotun primer compatible with the PU Ultra system.
Mixing: combine the two-component Jotafloor PU Ultra (Comp A : Comp B, 1:1 by volume) thoroughly before application.
Application: apply by roller, brush or squeegee at 150-200 micron dry film thickness per coat (165-220 micron wet film), achieving 90±2% solids by volume.
Anti-slip (optional): incorporate Jotafloor Non-Slip Aggregate into the wet film for enhanced slip resistance.
Curing: allow full cure per the technical data sheet before exposing to weathering or vehicular/pedestrian traffic.

full_name: Bostik Epcote-TXT
description: Bostik Epcote-TXT is a pre-pigmented, two-component, low-solvent epoxy resin-based textured coating for cement-based surfaces such as concrete and cement screeds. It is used in floor and wall applications subjected to normal to medium mechanical loads and aggressive chemicals, commonly specified for production, storage and assembly areas. Benefits include good abrasion and chemical resistance, resistance to sea/waste water, and steam-cleanable surfaces.
sow_steps: Surface preparation: sandblast concrete to remove laitance and dust (or thoroughly abrade with a hard wire brush / acid-etch with diluted hydrochloric acid as an alternative); wash off and dry thoroughly.
Moisture check: ensure surface dampness is below 4% before proceeding.
Primer mixing: mix Primer E2 Base and Hardener in a 3:2 ratio by weight after individually mixing each component.
Primer application: apply one brush coat of Primer E2 and allow to become tack-free before coating.
Coating mixing: mix Component A (base) and Component B (hardener) in a 9:1 ratio by weight using a slow-speed stirrer for 2 minutes.
Coating application: apply Bostik Epcote-TXT evenly by brush/roller; apply a second coat at right angles after the recommended interval to ensure full coverage.
Curing: allow minimum 7 days curing time; coated surface can then be steam cleaned.

full_name: Bostik HYTEC E736 Turbo (2K Epoxy Damp Proof Membrane / Primer System)
description: HYTEC E736 Turbo is a fast-curing, two-component epoxy resin damp-proof membrane used for damp-proofing concrete, cement screeds and old tiles prior to laying hard floor coverings or decorative finishes, forming a barrier against rising damp. Though primarily an epoxy DPM, it is commonly used as a fast-track priming/protection layer within Bostik's UAE flooring preparation systems ahead of coating or resin applications. It offers very low VOC emissions (EC1+, solvent-free) and a one-day protection solution.
application_areas: new and renovation concrete/cement screed floors
old tiled substrates prior to recoating
sow_steps: Surface preparation: ensure substrate is sound, clean and free of loose material; approved surfaces include concrete, cement screeds, precast concrete, old tiles.
Mixing: combine pre-measured Part A and Part B to a homogeneous medium-viscosity mixture.
Application: pour the mixed resin onto the subfloor and spread in one coat at 500-600 g/m² using a B2 spatula; pass a short-hair roller or spike roller to release air.
Sanding (optional): broadcast BOSTIK S409 sand directly over the wet resin if a sanded finish/key is required for subsequent adhesives.
Curing: allow to cure for approximately 4-5 hours at 20°C before covering or removing loose sand; do not leave epoxy DPM uncovered for more than 48 hours.
Follow-up primer: once cured, apply BOSTIK primer GRIP A936 XPRESS by lacquer or short-hair roller over the sanded surface.
Next steps: proceed with subsequent flooring/coating system as per project specification.

full_name: Henkel Polybit Polyscreed PU
description: Polyscreed PU is a four-component, water-based, chemical-resistant, antimicrobial polyurethane resin flooring system installed at 4-6mm thickness, developed under Henkel's Polybit range for the Middle East. It delivers a hard-wearing, abrasion resistant, smooth matt coloured floor finish with good chemical and water resistance, suitable for heavy-duty industrial flooring applications. Its antimicrobial formulation makes it suitable for hygiene-sensitive facilities.
sow_steps: Surface preparation: prepare and clean the substrate thoroughly, ensuring it is sound, dry and free of contamination.
Scratch coat: apply Polyscreed PU as a scratch coat at 1.6-2 kg/m² over the properly prepared surface; leave to dry completely.
Mixing: mix components using a slow-speed (approx. low rpm) drill for one minute to achieve homogeneous consistency.
Body coat pour: pour the mixed Polyscreed PU onto the floor within 24 hours of the scratch coat application.
Spreading: spread the mix with a notch trowel uniformly to achieve the desired thickness of 4-6mm.
De-airing: roll the material with a spike roller to release entrapped air from the mix.
Curing: allow to fully cure per technical data sheet before subjecting to traffic or chemical exposure.

full_name: BASF MasterTop 1210 CP
description: MasterTop 1210 CP is a multi-component, solvent-free epoxy coating system designed for application at 0.8-2.0mm thickness on basement and ground floor car parks or intermediate traffic decks with little or no structural movement. It provides a durable profiled finish with good wear and abrasion resistance, offering impermeable protection against vehicle-related spillages such as oils, greases, lubricants, aviation fuels and AC fluids. It also offers good general chemical resistance for various industrial applications.
application_areas: basement and ground floor car parks
sow_steps: Surface preparation: shot blast the concrete substrate to remove laitance and achieve a sound, clean profile.
Repair: fill cracks and surface defects with appropriate epoxy repair mortar.
Priming: apply MasterTop epoxy primer/resin coat to seal the substrate.
Broadcast coat: apply first coat of MasterTop 1210 CP and broadcast with MasterTop SRA aggregate to saturation; allow to cure 14-18 hours and sweep off excess aggregate.
Second/seal coat: apply a second coat of MasterTop 1210 CP at a minimum of 0.3-0.45 litres/m² to seal the aggregate and complete the wear surface.
Curing: allow to cure for a minimum of 48 hours before allowing traffic.

full_name: BASF MasterTop 1324
description: MasterTop 1324 is a seamless, self-smoothing, heavy-duty polyurethane-based flooring system applied at 2-4mm thickness, designed for protection of concrete floors subject to high levels of traffic, impact and abrasion. It is a high-performance PU resin floor system suitable for industrial use and demanding commercial environments requiring high scratch and wear resistance. It is commonly specified for loading bay areas and industrial flooring.
sow_steps: Surface preparation: shot blast or shot-peen the concrete substrate to achieve a sound, sufficiently roughened surface profile.
Repair: address cracks and surface defects using appropriate MasterTop/MasterEmaco repair mortars.
Priming: apply an appropriate MasterTop primer to the prepared substrate, ensuring full saturation and pore-free coverage.
Self-smoothing application: pour and spread MasterTop 1324 with a notched trowel or squeegee to achieve the specified 2-4mm thickness.
De-airing: pass a spiked/de-airing roller over the material shortly after application to remove entrapped air bubbles.
Curing: protect from moisture and traffic during initial cure; allow full cure per technical data sheet before opening to traffic.

full_name: BASF MasterTop 1230 Plus
description: MasterTop 1230 Plus is a single-coloured, self-smoothing, solvent-free epoxy overlay system designed to provide continuous protection for concrete floors at thicknesses of 2.0-4.0mm, often combined with waterproofing primers (e.g., MasterSeal P 385) in systems requiring moisture-tolerant application. It is dense and impervious, providing an ideal floor finish for food and beverage, pharmaceutical and chemical industries requiring both robust protection and a hygienic, long-lived floor. This makes it suitable for use as a protective membrane-backed coating system in wet processing environments.
sow_steps: Surface preparation: shot blast or shot-peen the concrete to achieve a suitable profile (min compressive strength class per BASF specification).
Moisture protection (if required): apply MasterSeal P 385 moisture-tolerant primer at approx. 1.5 kg/m² where substrate moisture or vapor barrier is a concern.
Priming: apply standard MasterTop primer to seal and prepare the substrate for the overlay.
Self-levelling coat application: pour and spread MasterTop 1230 Plus using a notched trowel or squeegee to 2.0-4.0mm thickness.
De-airing: pass a spiked roller over the material within minutes of application to release trapped air.
Curing: maintain ambient temperature within recommended range (8-30°C) and protect from moisture contact for at least 24 hours.
Final inspection: verify a dense, impervious, hygienic surface finish before handover.

full_name: BASF MasterTop 1325
description: MasterTop 1325 is a seamless, self-smoothing, comfort polyurethane flooring system with sound-absorbing and flexible properties, applied at 2-4mm thickness, offering high crack-bridging capability (Class A4) and a satin, antibacterial finish certified low-emission under AgBB standards. It is elastic (150% elongation) with good resistance to blood, alcohol, salts and cleaning/disinfection chemicals, and reduces impact noise by 2-4 dB. It is widely specified for offices, hospitals, schools, and commercial areas requiring both comfort and hygiene.
sow_steps: Surface preparation: shot blast or grind the concrete substrate to achieve minimum compressive strength class C20/25 and remove laitance/dust.
Moisture protection (if needed): apply MasterSeal P 385 primer at 1.5 kg/m² if substrate has rising damp or lacks a vapor barrier (max 4% moisture tolerance otherwise).
Primer application: mix and apply MasterTop P 615 primer with a rubber squeegee, finishing with a roller.
Base coat: mix MasterTop BC 325N components and apply with a notched trowel/spatula; pass a de-airing roller 5-10 minutes after application; protect from moisture for 24 hours.
Optional quartz filler: MasterTop F1 quartz filler (0.1-0.3mm) can be added up to 30% by weight to the resin for the self-levelling layer.
Top/finish coat: apply MasterTop TC 417W finishing layer using a microfibre roller, working in overlapping passes to avoid roller marks.
Curing: maintain ambient temperature between 8°C and 30°C during application, avoid direct sunlight, and allow full cure before use (typically several days).

full_name: Mapei Mapefloor I 500 W
description: Mapefloor I 500 W is a two-component, vapour-permeable, neutral-coloured epoxy formulation in water dispersion designed for industrial floors, which may be coloured with Mapecolor Paste. It can be applied as a 2mm thick smooth self-levelling coating over primed substrates or as part of a multi-layered non-slip system up to 5mm. This low-odour, water-based epoxy is well suited for industrial and commercial floor coatings where vapour permeability is beneficial.
sow_steps: Surface preparation: mechanically prepare (shot blast/grind) the concrete substrate to remove laitance and achieve a sound profile.
Priming: apply Mapecoat I 600 W primer to the substrate and allow to cure per manufacturer's schedule.
Mixing: combine Component A and Component B in a 1:12 ratio to achieve a fluid, neutral-coloured mix (pot life ~20 minutes at 20°C).

First layer application: apply Mapefloor I 500 W at 2-2.5 kg/m² using a notched or smooth trowel, or notched rake for multi-layer non-slip systems, broadcasting with Quartz aggregate (0.5-5mm) if needed.
Second layer (if multi-layer system): repeat with a second coat of Mapefloor I 500 W at 2-2.5 kg/m² with quartz broadcast for slip resistance.
Finishing layer: apply a final Mapefloor I 500 W finishing coat at 0.7 kg/m² for a smooth sealed surface.
Curing: allow to set to light foot traffic after 16 hours and fully harden within 7 days at 23°C/50% RH.

full_name: Mapei Mapefloor FC 200 ME
description: Mapefloor FC 200 ME is a two-component epoxy formulation with high solid content used to create multi-layered resin coatings with an attractive smooth or non-slip surface, formulated specifically for the Middle East (ME) market. It is used across chemical and pharmaceutical industries, foodstuffs industry facilities, car parks, and aseptic rooms. The system provides durable, chemically resistant coatings tailored to the region's climatic conditions.
sow_steps: Surface preparation: shot blast the concrete substrate to remove laitance, dust and contaminants, achieving a sound, dry surface.
Repair: fill cracks and defects with a suitable Mapei repair mortar.
Priming: apply an appropriate Mapei epoxy primer, ensuring uniform, pore-free coverage.
Mixing: combine Component A and B of Mapefloor FC 200 ME thoroughly using a low-speed mixer.
Base coat application: apply the mixed coating by roller or squeegee across the primed surface.
Non-slip broadcast (if specified): broadcast quartz aggregate into the wet coating for anti-slip texture.
Sealer/top coat: apply a final sealing coat over the broadcast aggregate to achieve the smooth or non-slip finish.
Curing: allow to cure fully per technical data sheet before subjecting to traffic or chemical exposure.

full_name: Mapei Mapefloor System CPU+/DP
description: Mapefloor System CPU+/DP is part of Mapei's polyurethane-cement (CPU) flooring range designed to create heavy-duty, damp-tolerant systems combining a waterproofing/damp-proof function with a polyurethane-cement wear layer. The CPU+ range is odourless during installation, suitable for the food and drinks industry, and offers excellent resistance to a wide range of chemicals such as oils, greases, acids, alkalis and solvents. It is engineered for the chemical, foodstuff and pharmaceutical industries where hygiene and chemical exposure protection are both critical.
sow_steps: Substrate preparation: ensure the cementitious substrate is solid, compact, stable, strong, sound and clean, appropriately dimensioned for anticipated loads.
Substrate strengthening (if required): apply Primer MF by roller in one or more coats until the substrate is fully saturated.
Priming/damp-proofing: apply an appropriate moisture-tolerant primer system to address any damp-proofing requirements before the CPU layer.
Scratch coat: apply Mapefloor CPU/DP mixed with Mapecolor CPU pigment using a straight steel trowel, skimmed to a feather edge.
Broadcast: fully broadcast the surface with Quartz 0.9 aggregate immediately after the scratch coat; once set, remove excess quartz and vacuum dust.
Self-levelling coat: apply the CPU self-levelling wear coat at 3-6mm thickness using a notched trowel or pin screed.
De-airing: spike roll the coating to remove entrapped air and produce a uniform surface.
Curing: allow appropriate cure time (typically several days) before full service use, particularly before exposure to thermal shock or heavy traffic.

full_name: Mapei Mapefloor CPU/MF
description: Mapefloor CPU/MF is a three-component polyurethane/cement-based formulate used to create protective coatings on industrial floors subjected to medium to heavy traffic and high chemical aggression, for dry and wet processing areas up to 70°C. It offers resistance to thermal shocks (hot water cleaning) and complies with foodstuffs sector standards (EN 1186, EN 13130). It is widely used in the chemical, pharmaceutical, food and beverage industries, including wineries, breweries and dairies.
sow_steps: Substrate preparation: ensure the cementitious screed is solid, compact, stable, strong, sound, clean and appropriately dimensioned for service loads.
Priming: apply Primer MF (for strengthening) or Primer SN mixed with 20-30% Quartz 0.5 for dry, cured substrates with moisture below 4%, poured and trowelled to a feather edge.
Broadcast (if scratch coat approach used): fully broadcast the primer/scratch coat with Quartz 0.9 immediately after application.
Surface cleanup: once primer/scratch coat has set, remove excess quartz, sand the surface and vacuum all dust.
Colour mixing: mix Mapefloor CPU/MF components A+B, then gradually add Mapecolor CPU pigment powder (5kg per kit) until homogeneous.
Self-levelling coat application: apply the pigmented CPU/MF mix at 1.7 kg/m² per mm of thickness (3-6mm total) using a notched trowel or pin screed.
De-airing and finishing: spike roll to remove air and achieve a lightly textured, durable, easy-to-clean surface.
Curing: allow to set to foot traffic within approximately 24 hours and reach full hardening within about 5-10 days depending on conditions.

full_name: Tremco Epoxy Primer (Protective Coatings range)
description: Tremco Epoxy Primer is a two-component (1:1 ratio), 100% solids epoxy primer used as a porous substrate primer and as a compatible tie-in layer connecting TREMproof below-grade waterproofing membranes, Vulkem urethane coatings and ExoAir air barrier membranes. It can also be used as a construction coat to temporarily protect substrates from moisture, or mixed with silica sand for shallow concrete repairs. Tremco's protective coatings range in the region also includes 100% solids epoxy floor/wall coatings impervious to acids, caustics, oils and various chemicals.
application_areas: concrete floors and walls in industrial facilities
protective coating tie-in layers for waterproofing systems
sow_steps: Surface preparation: ensure concrete/wood substrate is clean, sound and free of contaminants; remove dust, loose material and laitance.
Component conditioning: condition Part A and Part B components at 16-27°C for 24 hours prior to mixing.
Mixing: pre-mix Part A and Part B separately for approximately 1 minute each, then combine in a 1:1 ratio by volume and mix thoroughly for 3-5 minutes using a low-speed drill and paddle.
Application: apply Tremco Epoxy Primer by brush or short-nap roller at 10-30 mils thickness depending on vertical/horizontal application, ensuring full coverage.
Broadcast (if used as a base for membrane systems): apply a second coat at 10-12 mils followed by silica sand broadcast to refusal; remove loose sand once cured.
Overcoating: apply subsequent epoxy or urethane coatings once the primer becomes tack-free (typically within 8-16 hours at 24°C, no later than 24 hours after application).
Curing: allow full cure per technical data sheet before proceeding with subsequent flooring/waterproofing layers.

full_name: Tremco Vulkem 350NF (Traffic Deck Coating base coat)
description: Vulkem 350NF is a single-component, low-odour, low-VOC urethane membrane coating that bonds firmly to clean, dry concrete, plywood and metal, retaining integrity even with hairline substrate cracking up to 1.5mm. It forms the watertight base coat within Tremco's Vulkem traffic deck coating systems and is available in roller (R) and self-levelling (SL) grades for vertical and horizontal applications. It is a core component of Tremco's heavy-duty waterproof deck coating systems used across parking structures and trafficked decks.
sow_steps: Surface preparation: ensure substrate is clean, dry, and sound, free from oils, curing compounds and other contaminants.
Priming: apply Vulkem 171 Primer as an adhesion promoter for porous substrates such as concrete and wood.
Base coat mixing: prepare Vulkem 350NF (single component, moisture-curing) per manufacturer instructions.
Base coat application: apply Vulkem 350NF by roller (R grade) or self-levelling application (SL grade) to achieve a watertight membrane base coat.
Wear coat: apply a heavy-duty, aggregate-loaded epoxy wear coat (Vulkem Epoxy WC) over the cured base coat for abrasion resistance.
Top coat: apply an aesthetic urethane top coat (e.g., Vulkem 950NF) for UV stability and final colour finish.
Curing: allow appropriate cure time between coats and before trafficking, per Tremco's technical application instructions.

full_name: Tremco Vulkem 350NF/Epoxy WC/950NF Traffic Deck Coating System
description: The Vulkem 350NF/Epoxy WC/950NF system is a three-coat polyurethane traffic deck coating system featuring an integrated waterproof base membrane and an epoxy wear course, designed for extreme impact and abrasion resistance in the highest trafficked areas. It combines a watertight, crack-tolerant base coat (Vulkem 350NF), a heavy-duty aggregate-loaded epoxy wear coat, and an aesthetic, UV-stable urethane top coat (Vulkem 950NF). This heavy-duty waterproof deck coating system is engineered for demanding parking structure and podium deck applications.
sow_steps: Surface preparation: shot blast concrete to achieve a sound, contaminant-free profile suitable for waterproofing membrane adhesion.
Priming: apply Vulkem 171 Primer to enhance adhesion to the concrete substrate.
Base coat/waterproofing membrane: apply Vulkem 350NF as a watertight base coat, tolerant of hairline substrate cracking up to 1.5mm.
Wear coat: apply the heavy-duty, aggregate-loaded Vulkem Epoxy WC over the cured base coat for abrasion and impact resistance.
Aggregate broadcast (if specified): broadcast additional aggregate into the wear coat for enhanced slip resistance.
Top coat: apply Vulkem 950NF aesthetic urethane top coat for UV stability, gloss and colour retention.
Curing: allow full cure of each layer per Tremco's application instructions before opening to vehicular traffic.

full_name: Tremco CPG / Flowcrete Flowfresh HF PU Screed
description: Flowfresh HF is a HACCP International certified, antimicrobial-treated polyurethane resin floor screed, manufactured by Flowcrete (a brand of Tremco Construction Products Group), created in partnership with Polygiene to harness natural silver technology inhibiting bacterial growth. It offers a hard-wearing floor finish with a textured, coloured surface, ideal for wet processing zones such as food manufacturing and chemical processing plants. It carries HACCP International Certification and Gold Standard Eurofins Certification for Indoor Air Quality.
sow_steps: Surface preparation: shot blast the concrete/screed substrate (min 25 N/mm2) to remove laitance and contamination, ensuring dry, sound conditions.
Repair: fill any surface defects with an appropriate epoxy or PU repair mortar.
Priming: apply the Flowfresh primer system to the prepared substrate to promote adhesion.
Scratch coat: apply a scratch coat to seal the substrate and fill minor surface irregularities.
Screed application: trowel-apply the Flowfresh HF antimicrobial PU screed to the specified thickness using a notched trowel.
De-airing: spike-roll the screed to remove entrapped air and achieve a uniform, textured surface.
Sealer/top coat: apply a PU sealer coat for final hygienic, textured, low-slip finish.
Curing: allow full cure per manufacturer's technical data sheet before subjecting to full production traffic.

full_name: Sika-101A / Sika-101h Waterproof Cement (Crystalline/Cementitious Waterproofing Mortar)
description: Sika-101A (also marketed as Sika-101h) is a one-component, cement-based powder crystalline/cementitious waterproofing system for concrete and mortar substrates. When mixed with water it forms a slurry that is brush-applied to block capillary pores and form insoluble crystals that seal against water seepage under low to moderate hydrostatic pressure. It is widely used across the UAE for rigid, thin-layer waterproofing of wet areas, foundations and water-retaining structures.
sow_steps: Ensure substrate concrete/masonry is sound, clean, and free of laitance, oil, and loose particles.
Pre-saturate the substrate with clean water until saturated surface dry (SSD); remove standing water.
Mix Sika-101A powder with clean water to a lump-free, brushable slurry consistency using a slow-speed drill.
Apply the first coat by stiff brush or trowel at 1-2 mm thickness, working the slurry into the surface.
Allow the first coat to stiffen (typically 4-6 hours) before applying a second coat in a perpendicular direction.
Damp-cure the applied coats for 3-5 days, protecting from direct sun and rain during initial cure.
Allow full cure (5-7 days) before water tanking, backfilling, or tiling over the surface.

full_name: SikaTop® Seal-107 AE Two-Component Cementitious Waterproofing Slurry
description: SikaTop Seal-107 AE is a two-component, brush-applied, flexible cementitious waterproofing slurry combining polymer-modified cement with a liquid component, giving rigid but crack-tolerant waterproofing. It holds potable water approval, making it a market-leading choice in the UAE for bathrooms, water tanks, and foundation walls. It bonds well to concrete and masonry and can be applied on both positive and negative sides.
sow_steps: Prepare substrate: remove laitance, dust, and contaminants; repair defects with appropriate Sika repair mortar.
Saturate the substrate with water to achieve SSD condition prior to application.
Mix powder and liquid components mechanically at correct ratio until a homogeneous, lump-free slurry is achieved.
Apply the first coat by brush or trowel at the specified thickness (~1 mm per coat).
Reinforce movement/construction joints and corners with fillet and mesh where indicated.
Apply the second coat crosswise to the first once initial set has occurred (typically after 4-6 hours).
Cure under damp hessian or fine water spray for a minimum of 3 days; protect from rain within 24 hours of finishing.

full_name: SikaControl®-1200 WT Crystalline Waterproofing Admixture for Concrete and Mortars
description: SikaControl-1200 WT is a liquid, dual-mechanism integral crystalline waterproofing admixture dosed directly into the concrete mix. It reduces water permeability and imparts a self-healing capability to concrete in the presence of moisture, formulated to perform in hot and tropical GCC climatic conditions. It is used in structural concrete that is constantly or intermittently exposed to water.
sow_steps: Design the concrete mix ensuring water/cement ratio is below 0.5 for optimal performance.
Dose SikaControl-1200 WT into the concrete mix at the manufacturer-recommended rate during batching.
Combine with a Sika ViscoCrete/SikaPlast/Sikament superplasticizer if needed to control workability.
Batch and mix concrete thoroughly to ensure uniform admixture dispersion.
Place, compact and cure the concrete using standard good concrete practice; crystalline growth continues over time in the presence of moisture.
Combine with joint waterproofing products (Sika Waterbar, SikaFuko injection hose) at construction/movement joints for a complete system.

full_name: Sika Sarnafil® G 410 PVC Membrane (Polymeric Roof Waterproofing Membrane)
description: Sarnafil G 410 is a multi-layer, hot-air weldable, synthetic roof waterproofing sheet based on premium PVC with a glass non-woven inlay and UV stabilizers, engineered for direct sun exposure and hot climates. It offers proven long-term performance, root resistance and high dimensional stability, and is one of the most specified single-ply membrane systems for UAE flat roofs and roof decks. Although PVC (not PU) based, it is grouped in the Sika membrane range alongside PU-based Nitoproof-style liquid membranes for exposed roof waterproofing.
sow_steps: Inspect and prepare the roof deck/insulation substrate to be smooth, dry and free of sharp protrusions.
Install vapor control layer and thermal insulation boards as per system design.
Roll out Sarnafil G410 membrane sheets, allowing for correct overlap (typically 80-120 mm).
Mechanically fasten or adhere the membrane to the substrate per the chosen fixing method (Sarnafast fasteners or Sarnacol adhesive).
Hot-air weld all membrane overlaps/seams using automatic or hand welding equipment; verify weld continuity by probe testing.
Install membrane detailing at upstands, penetrations, drains and parapets using Sarnafil accessories.
Carry out final flood/hose test and visual inspection before handover.

full_name: SikaShield® W-159 ED Wet-Applied Modified Bitumen Waterproofing Membrane
description: SikaShield W-159 ED is a double-sided, self-adhesive/wet-applied modified bitumen waterproofing membrane consisting of a cross-laminated PE film with two modified bitumen layers protected by silver release liners. Part of the SikaShield APP/SBS bituminous membrane portfolio, it is designed for below-grade waterproofing where the membrane is placed prior to structural concrete pour, bonding directly to fresh concrete. SikaShield P (APP) membranes are especially suited to hot GCC climates due to their excellent heat resistance.
sow_steps: Prepare and compact the blinding/lean concrete surface (typically 50 mm, 20 MPa) to a smooth, even level.
Lay out SikaShield W-159 ED membrane rolls over the blinding concrete, overlapping edges by the specified width.
Remove release liners and press the membrane firmly to achieve full contact/adhesion, or torch-apply per membrane grade.
Seal all laps and overlaps continuously; detail upstands, corners and penetrations with additional membrane strips.
Protect the membrane with a protection board or screed prior to reinforcement fixing to avoid puncture during rebar works.
Pour structural concrete directly over the membrane, allowing it to bond integrally with the new concrete.
Inspect for punctures/damage prior to concrete pour and repair immediately with patch membrane.

full_name: Sikalastic® 152 AE Crack-Bridging Elastomeric Cementitious Waterproofing Membrane
description: Sikalastic 152 AE is a two-component, polyurethane-modified, crack-bridging cementitious waterproofing membrane, widely specified in the UAE for below-grade structures, podium decks, and wet areas. It combines the ease of cementitious application with elastomeric flexibility to bridge dynamic and static cracks in the substrate. It is typically combined with Sika Waterbar at construction joints for a complete basement waterproofing system.
sow_steps: Prepare substrate: remove laitance/contaminants and pre-wet concrete to SSD condition.
Treat construction/movement joints with Sika Waterbar or reinforcing mesh before slurry coats.
Mix Sikalastic 152 AE powder and liquid components mechanically to a homogeneous slurry.
Apply first coat by brush, roller, or spray at the specified wet film thickness.
Embed reinforcing mesh in the wet first coat at corners, joints and crack-prone zones.
Apply second coat in a perpendicular direction after the first coat has cured sufficiently (typically 4-6 hours).
Cure for the recommended period (3-5 days) before backfilling, tiling, or flood testing.

full_name: Sikalastic® 560 GCC Eco-Friendly Liquid-Applied Roof Waterproofing Membrane
description: Sikalastic 560 GCC is a one-component, water-based, liquid-applied waterproofing membrane based on Sika's Co-Elastic Technology (polyurethane-modified acrylic dispersion). It is cold-applied, requires no mixing, and forms a seamless, highly elastic, UV-resistant coating validated for 45°C+ UAE conditions. The white variant offers SRI ≥ 90 for cool-roof and heat-island mitigation compliance.
sow_steps: Ensure the roof substrate is clean, sound, dry and free of loose material, oil, or grease.
Apply a compatible Sika primer (e.g. Sika Primer MB) where required for porous or bituminous substrates.
Apply Sikalastic 560 GCC directly from the pail using roller, brush, or airless spray in the first coat.
Allow the first coat to dry (1-2 hours at UAE ambient temperatures above 40°C) before recoating.
Apply the second coat at right angles to the first to ensure full, pinhole-free coverage.
Detail upstands, drains, and penetrations with reinforced fabric embedded in the coating.
Allow full cure (approx. 6 days) before exposing to foot traffic or ponding water.

full_name: Sika® Injection-101 RC / Sika® Injection-101 AP Polyurethane Injection Resin
description: Sika Injection-101 AP/RC are two-part, low-viscosity, fast-foaming, water-reactive polyurethane injection resins used to stop active water intrusion in cracks, joints, and cavities in concrete, masonry, and natural stone. On contact with water they expand up to 40 times to form a dense flexible foam, sealing leak paths under pressure. They are also used in specialist applications such as bored/sheet pile walls, anchor heads, and micro-tunneling.
sow_steps: Identify the leak path and drill injection ports (packers) along the crack or joint at specified spacing and angle.
Flush ports with water to verify flow path and clean the injection channel.
Mix Part A and Part B components slowly and thoroughly for at least 1 minute (max 250 rpm) until fully blended.
Transfer the mixed resin into the injection pump feed container and use within the pot life.
Inject the resin through packers starting from the lowest point, progressing upward, monitoring for resin emerging at adjacent ports.
Allow the resin to react and foam on contact with water, sealing the leak path.
Remove packers and patch injection points with an appropriate repair mortar; verify watertightness after curing.

full_name: Fosroc Renderoc HS High-Strength Cementitious Repair/Waterproofing Render
description: Fosroc Renderoc HS is a single-component, polymer-modified, cementitious repair and rendering mortar used across UAE concrete repair and waterproofing applications. It is part of the Renderoc range distributed in the UAE by Al Gurg Fosroc, and offers durability, low permeability, and good bond strength for structural surface reinstatement combined with waterproofing performance.
sow_steps: Remove all deteriorated, weak, or contaminated concrete back to sound substrate; expose and treat corroding reinforcement.
Clean the prepared surface of dust, oil, and loose particles; saturate with water to SSD condition.
Mix Renderoc HS with the specified quantity of clean water using a slow-speed drill until a lump-free consistency is achieved.
Apply the mortar by hand or trowel in layers up to the maximum recommended thickness per coat.
Finish with a wood float or steel trowel to the required surface texture.
Cure the applied render with damp hessian, curing compound, or polythene sheeting for the recommended period.
Apply a compatible waterproofing coating/membrane over the cured render where full waterproofing is required.

full_name: Fosroc Brushbond Crystalline (Crystalline Capillary Waterproofing Slurry)
description: Brushbond Crystalline is a cementitious, crystalline waterproof coating that protects concrete structures by forming insoluble crystals inside capillaries and micro-cracks in the presence of moisture. Unlike surface-barrier membranes, it continues generating crystalline growth over time, providing long-lasting impermeability even under hydrostatic pressure. It is applied in two coats and is suited to both new and old concrete, above or below ground.
sow_steps: Remove all dust, loose particles, grease and curing compounds from the substrate.
Saturate the concrete surface to achieve saturated surface-dry (SSD) condition before application.
Add Brushbond Crystalline powder to clean water and mix with a slow-speed drill for 3-5 minutes until lump-free.
Apply the first coat using a stiff brush at a coverage rate of 1.0-1.5 kg/m².
Allow 4-6 hours before applying the second coat in a crosswise direction.
Damp-cure the surface for 5-7 days after the final coat, protecting from rain for at least 24 hours.
Pre-treat joints and cracks larger than 0.4 mm separately, as the crystalline slurry alone is not sufficient for wide cracks.

full_name: Fosroc Nitoproof® 800 Polyurethane Liquid-Applied Waterproofing & Crack-Bridging Membrane
description: Fosroc Nitoproof 800 is a polyurethane-based, liquid-applied waterproofing and crack-bridging protection system suitable for new and existing structures. In exposed applications, it is used as a system with Nitoprime UR FS primer and Nitoproof UVR Topcoat, providing a seamless, durable, UV- and weather-resistant, slip-resistant, and chemical-resistant finish. In below-ground situations, the membrane can be used stand-alone.
sow_steps: Prepare the substrate to be clean, sound, dry, and free of laitance, oil, and loose material.
Apply Fosroc Nitoprime UR FS primer at the recommended coverage rate for exposed applications.
Allow the primer to become tack-free per manufacturer guidance before membrane application.
Apply Nitoproof 800 Membrane by roller, brush, or squeegee in the first coat at the specified wet film thickness.
Apply a second coat once the first has cured sufficiently, achieving full seamless coverage.
For exposed roofs, finish with Nitoproof UVR Topcoat to provide UV stability and color finish.
For below-ground use, apply the membrane as a standalone system directly to the substrate without topcoat.

full_name: Fosroc Proofex Torchseal 3P and 4P Torch-Applied APP Bituminous Membrane
description: Proofex Torchseal 3P and 4P are torch-applied APP (atactic polypropylene) based bituminous membranes manufactured from heavily modified bitumen, reinforced with a non-woven polyester fabric core weighing 180 g/m². They are rot-proof, malleable, and designed for waterproofing of building and civil engineering structures, and are a standard specification for basement and roof torch-on waterproofing across the UAE.
sow_steps: Prepare the substrate to be smooth, dry, and free of sharp protrusions, applying a bituminous primer (Proofex Torchseal Primer) first.
Allow the primer to become tack-free (typically 1-2 hours depending on ambient temperature).
Position and align the Torchseal membrane roll, ensuring correct overlap of side and end laps (typically 100 mm min).
Torch-apply heat evenly to the underside of the membrane using a propane torch while unrolling, melting the bitumen to bond to the substrate.
Seal and weld all overlaps by heating and pressing with a trowel to ensure a continuous bitumen bead is visible at the seams.
Detail upstands, penetrations and drains with additional membrane pieces and torch-welded flashing.
Carry out a flood test or visual inspection to confirm continuity before covering with protection screed or insulation.

full_name: Fosroc Brushbond Acrylic Polymer-Modified Elastomeric Waterproofing Membrane Coating
description: Brushbond is a two-component, acrylic polymer-modified, elastomeric waterproofing membrane coating consisting of Brushbond powder and a liquid acrylic emulsion. It provides a seamless waterproof coating suitable for water tanks, reservoirs, swimming pools, roofs, toilets and wet areas, resisting positive hydrostatic pressure up to 7 meters head, and is negligible-effect tested for potable water contact (BS 6920).
application_areas: water tanks and reservoirs (potable water approved)
sow_steps: Prepare the substrate by removing laitance, dust, and contaminants; dampen the surface prior to application.
Mix Brushbond powder and liquid acrylic emulsion components in the correct proportion until an easily brushable coating is produced.
Apply the coating using a stiff brush or trowel to the desired thickness, working into honeycombed or pitted areas.
Apply subsequent coats crosswise once the previous coat has set (drying time approximately 4-6 hours at normal temperatures).
Reinforce corners, joints and cracks with additional coating thickness as required.
Cure the coating for the recommended period before filling tanks or exposing to water immersion.
Verify coverage uniformity, particularly at corners and upstands, before handover.

full_name: Fosroc Proofex GP Cold-Applied Bituminous Waterproofing Membrane
description: Fosroc Proofex GP is a cold-applied, brush/roller-applied bituminous waterproofing membrane designed for building and civil engineering structures. It offers fast application without the need for torch equipment and provides good adhesion to primed concrete, making it suitable for smaller-scale or detailing waterproofing works where hot works are restricted on site.
sow_steps: Prepare the substrate to be clean, sound and dry, removing loose material and laitance.
Apply a compatible bituminous primer and allow to become tack-free.
Apply Proofex GP by brush or roller in a uniform coat at the recommended coverage rate.
Allow the first coat to cure before applying a second coat, if required for full waterproofing thickness.
Reinforce corners, joints, and penetrations with fabric embedded in the wet coating.
Allow full cure before backfilling or covering with protection board.
Inspect coated surface for pinholes or thin spots and touch up as necessary.

full_name: Fosroc Renderoc Injection Grouting System for Crack and Joint Waterproofing
description: Fosroc's grouting range (used alongside Brushbond/Proofex systems) provides cementitious and resin-based injection grouts for sealing cracks, joints and voids against water ingress in concrete structures. These products are used to stop active leaks and fill honeycombed or defective concrete prior to surface waterproofing coatings being applied, forming a standard part of UAE basement remediation packages.
sow_steps: Locate and mark the leak path or defective joint/crack requiring injection.
Drill injection ports along the crack/joint at the specified spacing and angle, intersecting the crack plane.
Flush the crack/port with water to confirm the flow path and clean out debris.
Mix the injection resin/grout per manufacturer instructions to the correct consistency.
Inject from the lowest port upward using a manual or pneumatic injection pump until refusal or resin emerges from adjacent ports.
Allow the grout/resin to cure and seal the water path.
Remove injection packers, patch ports with repair mortar, and apply a surface waterproof coating if specified.

full_name: MasterSeal® 581 (formerly Thoroseal) Portland-Cement Based Waterproof Coating
description: MasterSeal 581 is a Portland-cement based coating for concrete and masonry that resists both positive and negative hydrostatic pressure, with potable water approval (BS 6920, NSF 61). It provides rigid cementitious waterproofing for below-grade surfaces such as basements, retaining walls and foundations, and is part of BASF's broader MasterSeal 580-series (581 standard coating, 582 foundation coating, 584 plaster mix).
sow_steps: Prepare the substrate by removing dust, laitance, and surface contaminants; ensure surface is sound.
Pre-wet the concrete/masonry substrate to achieve an SSD condition.
Mix MasterSeal 581 powder with clean water to a lump-free brushable consistency.
Apply the first coat by stiff brush, working the material into the pores of the substrate.
Allow initial set (approx. 10 minutes) and final set (approx. 90 minutes at 21°C) between coats as specified.
Apply a second coat to achieve the full specified thickness and coverage.
Moist-cure the coating for the recommended period before backfilling or exposing to water.

full_name: MasterSeal® 588 Waterproofing and Elastomeric Cementitious Coating for Concrete and Masonry
description: MasterSeal 588 is a two-component, ready-to-use elastomeric coating combining Portland cement/quartz sand powder with an acrylic polymer emulsion liquid, curing to a flexible waterproof membrane. It resists both positive and negative water pressure, retains flexibility when submerged, and is approved for potable water contact, making it a leading UAE choice for water reservoirs, bathrooms, kitchens, and basements, with reinforcing mesh available for enhanced crack-bridging.
sow_steps: Prepare the substrate: clean and pre-wet concrete/masonry to a damp, saturated surface-dry condition.
Mix Part A (powder) into Part B (liquid) using a low-speed drill until a homogeneous slurry is formed; avoid overmixing.
Apply the base coat by brush, roller, or spray at approx. 1.2 kg/m², keeping each layer under 1 mm thick.
Embed MasterSeal 588 Mesh into the wet base coat at all construction joints and cracks, with minimum 200 mm width coverage.
Apply a second/third coat crosswise over the mesh, achieving full encapsulation and target coverage (~1.2 kg/m² per layer).
Allow the coating to cure a minimum of 5 days at 20°C before exposing to water or backfilling.
Protect the freshly applied coating from rain, strong wind, and direct sun during curing.

full_name: MasterSeal® 345 Spray-Applied Sandwich Waterproofing Membrane
description: MasterSeal 345 is a spray-applied polymeric (ethylene-vinyl acetate copolymer with rapid-hardening cement) waterproofing membrane used in a sandwich construction between layers of sprayed or cast-in-situ concrete, primarily for tunnels and underground structures. It forms a fully bonded monolithic system that prevents lateral water migration at the concrete-membrane interface and has demonstrated resistance to water pressures up to 15-20 bar in laboratory testing.
sow_steps: Prepare the first layer of sprayed or cast concrete substrate, ensuring it is clean and free of loose material.
Set up spray equipment calibrated for MasterSeal 345 powder-to-water ratio (30-50% water addition by product weight).
Spray-apply MasterSeal 345 evenly across the substrate to the specified thickness (typically 3-10 mm).
Allow the membrane to chemically harden (4-6 hours depending on ambient conditions).
Apply the second structural layer of sprayed or cast concrete directly onto the cured membrane to complete the sandwich system.
Inspect for full coverage and continuity at construction joints and complex geometry areas (niches, branches).
Coordinate with drainage systems where water ingress is present, as MasterSeal 345 cannot be applied to actively running water surfaces.

full_name: MasterSeal® HLM 5000 R Liquid Cold-Applied Polyurethane Waterproofing Membrane
description: MasterSeal HLM 5000 R is a single-component, moisture-curing, polyurethane-based liquid waterproofing membrane that cures by reaction with atmospheric moisture to form a tough, seamless, elastomeric membrane. Available in self-leveling and roller grades, it protects structures from water ingress with high elongation (>500%) and good tensile strength, suited to a wide range of horizontal and vertical waterproofing applications.
sow_steps: Prepare substrate to be clean, dry, sound, and free of contaminants, laitance, and sharp protrusions.
Apply a compatible primer (e.g., MasterSeal P primer) where required by substrate porosity.
Apply MasterSeal HLM 5000 R self-leveling grade for horizontal surfaces or roller grade for vertical surfaces.
Apply in two coats to reach target dry film thickness, allowing tack-free time between coats (under 12 hours at 23°C).
Reinforce detailing at penetrations, drains, and upstands with fabric embedded in the membrane.
Allow full cure (under 24 hours at 23°C) prior to flood testing or covering with protection/insulation.
Conduct a flood test before installing overburden, pavers, or screed.

full_name: MasterSeal® 590 Waterstop for Construction and Movement Joints
description: MasterSeal 590 is part of BASF's joint waterproofing range used in conjunction with MasterSeal cementitious coatings to seal construction and movement joints against water ingress, often specified alongside injection systems for below-grade structures. It complements the MasterSeal 581/582/584 waterproof coating system in below-grade specifications, forming a complete joint sealing and waterproofing package for basements and foundations.
sow_steps: Identify and clean all construction and movement joints to receive the waterstop system.
Position and secure the waterstop centrally within the joint per design detail before concrete pour.
Ensure continuous, unbroken waterstop runs at all changes of direction and intersections using factory or field-welded joints.
Pour and compact concrete carefully around the waterstop to avoid displacement or honeycombing.
For post-construction leak remediation, supplement with injection grouting at the joint if water ingress is detected.
Apply MasterSeal cementitious coating over the joint area for redundant surface waterproofing.
Inspect and pressure/flood test the joint after curing.

full_name: MAPELASTIC Two-Component Flexible Cementitious Waterproofing Membrane
description: Mapelastic is a two-component coating based on cementitious binders, fine-grained selected aggregates, additives, and synthetic polymers dispersed in water. The cured layer remains flexible under all environmental conditions and is waterproof up to 1.5 bar pressure, resistant to deicing salts, sulfates, chlorides, and CO2. It complies with EN 14891 and EN 1504-2/9, and is widely used across the UAE for balconies, terraces, bathrooms, and swimming pools before tiling.
sow_steps: Prepare the substrate to be clean, sound, and free of dust, oil, and loose particles; repair defects beforehand.
Pre-wet the substrate until saturated but not glistening with surface water.
Mix the powder and liquid components of Mapelastic using a low-speed mixer until homogeneous.
Spray or trowel-apply at least two coats at a minimum 1 mm thickness per coat using a rendering machine or brush.
Embed Mapenet 150 alkali-resistant mesh in the first coat at corners, joints, and change-of-plane details.
Smooth and level the material to form a final thickness of at least 2 mm.
Cure for the recommended period before tiling with a suitable Mapei adhesive.

full_name: MAPELASTIC ZERO Flexible Two-Component Cementitious Waterproofing Membrane
description: Mapelastic Zero is a flexible, two-component cementitious waterproofing membrane engineered for durable, quick, and guaranteed waterproofing of balconies, terraces, bathrooms, and swimming pools. It remains flexible down to -20°C and eliminates water infiltrations that cause structural damage and reduce building lifespan; it is easy to mix and apply and must be finished with ceramic or natural stone tiles.
sow_steps: Ensure the substrate is clean, structurally sound, and free of contaminants.
Pre-wet the substrate lightly before application to control absorption.
Mix Component A (powder) and Component B (liquid) using a low-speed drill to a homogeneous, lump-free consistency.
Apply the first coat by trowel or brush across the full surface, including upstands.
Embed reinforcing mesh at movement joints, corners and pipe penetrations.
Apply a second coat perpendicular to the first once initial cure has occurred.
Allow full cure before proceeding directly to tile installation with compatible Mapei tile adhesive.

full_name: Mapeproof® Liquid Membrane / Mapelastic AquaDefense Ready-to-Use Liquid Waterproofing Membrane
description: Mapelastic AquaDefense (marketed regionally alongside the Mapeproof line) is a ready-to-use, flexible, solvent-free liquid waterproofing membrane applied directly from the pail without mixing. It is designed for fast-track waterproofing of wet areas and structures prior to tiling, providing a seamless elastomeric barrier resistant to water and vapor transmission.
sow_steps: Prepare the substrate to be clean, dry, and free of dust and loose material.
Apply the ready-mixed liquid membrane directly by roller or brush without dilution.
Reinforce corners, joints, and pipe penetrations with fabric tape embedded in the wet membrane.
Apply a second coat once the first coat has dried to a uniform film (typically 1-4 hours depending on conditions).
Ensure full, pinhole-free coverage, checking film build with a wet film thickness gauge.
Allow the membrane to cure fully before proceeding with tile installation or backfilling.
Conduct a water test on critical wet areas prior to handover.

full_name: MONOLASTIC One-Component Elastic Cementitious Waterproofing Membrane
description: Monolastic is a one-component, elastic, cementitious waterproofing membrane used as a waterproofing treatment on balconies, terraces, bathrooms, showers, and swimming pools before installing floor or wall coverings. It is EN 1504-2 certified for repair, maintenance and protection of concrete structures, and EN 14891 certified as a waterproofing product applied under floor and wall coverings, offering pore-blocking performance similar to crystalline-type systems in a ready-mixed one-component format.
sow_steps: Prepare the substrate ensuring it is sound, clean and free of dust or grease.
Dampen the substrate lightly prior to application to regulate absorption.
Mix Monolastic powder with clean water using a low-speed drill to a homogeneous, brushable consistency.
Apply the first coat evenly by brush or trowel across the whole surface, including upstands and corners.
Reinforce corners and joints with fiberglass mesh embedded in the first coat.
Apply the second coat once the first has set, achieving the total specified thickness.
Cure per manufacturer guidance before proceeding to tile installation.

full_name: TREMproof® 201/60 High-Solids Modified Polyurethane Waterproofing Membrane
description: TREMproof 201/60 is a high-solids, VOC-compliant, modified polyurethane waterproofing membrane available in self-leveling, roller, and trowel grades. It is a one-part, moisture-curing elastomer designed for use on backfilled walls and split-slab applications, primarily on concrete and masonry, and can be applied to damp and green concrete, making it suitable for fast-track UAE construction schedules.
sow_steps: Prepare substrate to be structurally sound, clean, and free of laitance, oil, and standing water.
Apply the recommended Tremco primer to porous substrates and allow to dry per TDS timing.
Apply TREMproof 201/60 in the appropriate grade (self-leveling for horizontal, roller/trowel for vertical or detailing).
Achieve the specified wet film thickness in a single or multiple coats as required by project specification.
Detail all penetrations, corners, and drains with reinforcing fabric embedded in the membrane.
Allow the membrane to fully cure (moisture-cure) before backfilling or applying protection board.
Install drainage composite and protection board prior to backfill to prevent membrane damage.

full_name: TREMproof® 260 Single-Component Asphalt Emulsion Waterproofing Membrane
description: TREMproof 260 is a polymer-enhanced, single-component, fluid-applied asphalt emulsion, below-grade waterproofing membrane that can be applied to damp or green concrete, allowing faster application after formwork removal or following wet weather. Available in spray and roller grades, it is commonly used for foundation walls, retaining walls, and most backfilled applications in UAE substructure works.
sow_steps: Prepare the substrate to be free of form-release residues, laitance, and sharp protrusions.
Apply TREMproof 260 by airless spray or roller in the first coat at the recommended wet film thickness.
Allow the first coat to cure/dry to a tack-free state before recoating.
Apply a second coat to achieve the specified total dry film thickness.
Reinforce corners, penetrations, and cold joints with integrated fabric reinforcement.
Install drainage board and protection course prior to backfilling.
Backfill carefully to avoid puncturing or damaging the cured membrane.

full_name: TREMproof® 6100 Hot-Applied Rubberized Asphalt Waterproofing Membrane
description: TREMproof 6100 is a one-part, 100% solids, hot-applied, multi-layered, fabric-reinforced rubberized asphalt waterproofing membrane. It is used extensively for critical below-grade waterproofing where a monolithic, seamless, high-build membrane is required, offering excellent resistance to hydrostatic pressure for basements and below-grade structures.
sow_steps: Prepare the substrate to be smooth, dry, and free of sharp protrusions or debris.
Heat the rubberized asphalt material in a mechanically agitated melter to the manufacturer-specified application temperature.
Apply the hot rubberized asphalt to the substrate at the specified thickness using a notched squeegee or mechanical applicator.
Embed reinforcing fabric within the membrane build for added tensile strength as specified.
Apply subsequent layers to build up to the full specified membrane thickness while material is still hot.
Install a protection board immediately over the finished membrane before backfilling.
Inspect for full coverage and pinhole-free continuity prior to backfill.

full_name: Tremco Aqua-Dam U Hydrophobic Polyurethane Injection Resin
description: Tremco Aqua-Dam U is a hydrophobic polyurethane compound injected into concrete and other sound substrates to stop active water infiltration. It reacts with water to form a closed-cell foam that seals cracks and joints under pressure, used widely for remedial leak-stopping works in UAE basements and water-retaining structures.
sow_steps: Identify the active leak source and mark injection points along the crack or joint.
Drill injection ports at the specified spacing and angle to intersect the water path.
Insert injection packers and flush with water to verify flow and clean the channel.
Prepare the Aqua-Dam U resin per manufacturer mixing instructions.
Inject the resin under pressure using a single or dual-component pump, starting from the lowest point.
Allow the resin to react with water and expand to seal the leak path.
Remove packers, patch injection points, and verify watertightness.

full_name: Polyglas High-Performance Prefabricated Modified Bitumen Waterproofing Membrane
