description: Polyglas is an exceptionally high-performance, prefabricated modified bitumen membrane consisting of a proprietary waterproofing compound reinforced with a double reinforcement of spunbonded non-woven polyester and fiberglass mat. It is available in black smooth polyethylene-surfaced finish for covered applications or coloured mineral granule surfacing for exposed applications, and is distributed in the UAE market (including via Bitumat) for torch-applied roofing and waterproofing.
sow_steps: Prepare the substrate with a compatible bituminous primer and allow to become tack-free.
Position the Polyglas membrane roll, aligning for correct side and end overlaps.
Torch-apply heat to the underside of the membrane while unrolling to melt the bitumen compound and bond to substrate.
Weld and seal all overlaps by heating and pressing to achieve a continuous bitumen bleed line.
Detail penetrations, drains, and upstands with additional torch-welded membrane pieces.
For exposed applications, ensure mineral granule surface is fully bonded and uniform.
Inspect the completed membrane for continuity before covering with insulation, screed, or ballast.

full_name: POLYLASTIC One-Pack Water-Based Elastomeric Bitumen Liquid Membrane
description: Polylastic is a ready-to-use, one-pack, water-based elastomeric bitumen membrane made from a blend of special bitumen, synthetic elastomeric resins and high-dispersion fillers, offering high adhesion on many substrates. Once dry, it forms a highly elastic film resistant to UV radiation and hairline substrate cracking, compliant with EN 14891 and EN 1504-2, and can be left exposed, covered with paving, or used to restore old polymer-bitumen membranes.
sow_steps: Ensure the substrate (concrete, fibre-cement, wood, metal, or old bitumen membrane) is clean and dry.
Stir Polylastic to an even, fluid consistency before use, as it is supplied ready-mixed.
Apply the first coat using a brush, roller, or trowel across the full surface.
Allow the first coat to dry for a minimum of 24 hours before proceeding.
Apply the second coat, ensuring full and even coverage with no pinholes.
Where paving is to be laid on top, allow the membrane to cure fully before installing the wearing course.
Store and use within shelf life (12 months) and avoid application below 5°C.

full_name: POLYSINT SUN REFLECT Water-Based Fibre-Reinforced Acrylic Liquid Membrane
description: Polysint Sun Reflect is a water-based, fibre-reinforced acrylic liquid membrane in white offering high solar reflectivity (SRI 105, 83% solar reflectance, 91% thermal emissivity), used to protect bitumen waterproofing membranes and concrete surfaces from UV degradation and heat gain. It is well suited to UAE cool-roof strategies to reduce rooftop surface temperatures and extend the service life of underlying waterproofing systems.
application_areas: exposed roof slabs (protective coating over bitumen membranes)
sow_steps: Ensure the underlying waterproofing membrane or concrete surface is clean, dry, and free of dust.
Stir the product to a uniform consistency before application.
Apply the first coat by brush, roller, or spray at the recommended coverage rate (700 g/m² on smooth membranes, ~1000 g/m² on shingled membranes).
Allow the first coat to dry before applying the second coat.
Apply the second coat to achieve full, even, reflective coverage (total ~2-2.5 kg/m² when used as standalone waterproofing).
Avoid application in freezing conditions or on membranes with active moisture.
Inspect for uniform white reflective finish and touch up any thin areas.

full_name: Bostik BLOCK X911 Terra Turbo Light 2K Bitumen-Free Waterproofing Membrane
description: BLOCK X911 Terra Turbo Light 2K is a two-component, bitumen-free waterproofing membrane designed as a flexible, crack-bridging under-tile waterproofing system. It is part of Bostik's Seal & Block wet-room waterproofing range used in UAE bathrooms and wet areas, applied together with sealing tapes for corners and joints prior to tile installation.
sow_steps: Ensure the substrate is clean, sound, and free of dust, grease, and loose particles.
Apply sealing tape to all internal corners and wall/floor junctions before the main membrane coat.
Mix the two components of BLOCK X911 Terra Turbo Light 2K per manufacturer ratio to a homogeneous paste.
Apply the first coat of membrane by brush, roller, or trowel across the entire wet area, over the sealing tape.
Embed additional reinforcement at pipe penetrations and drain collars as needed.
Apply a second coat once the first is touch-dry, ensuring uniform coverage and thickness.
Allow to cure fully before installing tiles with a flexible tile adhesive such as Ardacolor Flex grout.

full_name: Bostik Boscoseal PU-X One-Component Polyurethane Liquid-Applied Waterproofing Membrane
description: Bostik Boscoseal PU-X is a one-component, ready-to-use, highly elastic, cold-applied polyurethane waterproofing membrane that cures into a seamless membrane with excellent abrasion, mechanical, chemical, thermal, and UV resistance. It offers crack-bridging capability ≥2 mm and can be applied over old bituminous membranes and asphalt felts (APP/SBS), PVC, and EPDM using special primers, making it a versatile UAE refurbishment solution.
application_areas: roof refurbishment over old bitumen/PVC/EPDM membranes
sow_steps: Prepare the substrate to be clean, dry, and free of dust, oil, and loose material.
Apply the appropriate primer if bonding over old bituminous, PVC, or EPDM membranes.
Apply Boscoseal PU-X directly from the container using roller, brush, or squeegee without mixing.
Apply the first coat at the recommended wet film thickness across the full surface.
Reinforce joints, upstands, and penetrations with fabric embedded into the wet membrane.
Apply a second coat once the first has cured sufficiently for full, seamless coverage.
Finish with Boscoseal Top Coat where UV-stable, aesthetic topcoat finish is required.

full_name: Bostik CemenTech C2 Extra Two-Component Extra Flexible Waterproofing Mortar
description: CemenTech C2 Extra is a two-component, extra flexible cementitious waterproofing mortar with high adhesion quality that strongly adheres to substrates, offers high abrasion resistance, and is suitable for use with drinking water. It is part of Bostik's core UAE waterproofing range for wet areas and water-retaining structures requiring flexible cementitious protection.
sow_steps: Prepare the substrate to be sound, clean, and free of dust, grease, and loose material.
Pre-wet the substrate to achieve a damp, saturated surface-dry condition.
Mix the powder and liquid components of CemenTech C2 Extra to a homogeneous, lump-free slurry.
Apply the first coat by brush or trowel over the full surface, including upstands.
Embed reinforcing mesh at corners, joints, and pipe penetrations while the first coat is still wet.
Apply the second coat crosswise once the first has cured sufficiently.
Cure for the recommended period before filling with water or proceeding to tile installation.

full_name: PREPRUFE® 300R Plus Pre-Applied Waterproofing Membrane
description: Preprufe 300R Plus is a pre-applied waterproofing membrane that bonds integrally to poured concrete using GCP's Advanced Bond Technology, forming a continuous adhesive bond that prevents lateral water migration. It is a heavy-duty 46-mil composite sheet of HDPE film, pressure-sensitive adhesive, and weather-resistant coating, designed for horizontal use below slabs and rafts (mud slabs) with primer-less ZipLap seams for reliable installation.
sow_steps: Prepare the blinding/mud slab to be smooth, clean, and free of sharp protrusions.
Roll out Preprufe 300R Plus membrane sheets directly onto the prepared substrate.
Overlap adjacent sheets using the dual-adhesive ZipLap seam system, ensuring adhesive-to-adhesive bond without heat or specialized tools.
Detail penetrations and terminations with Preprufe Tape and Bituthene Liquid Membrane for continuous membrane adhesion.
Protect the installed membrane from damage during reinforcement fixing using plywood or protection boards where required.
Pour structural concrete directly onto the membrane, allowing the Advanced Bond Technology to create an integral bond as the concrete cures.
Inspect for punctures or seam separation prior to concrete pour and repair immediately.

full_name: BITUTHENE® 4000 Self-Adhered Rubberized Asphalt Waterproofing Membrane System
description: Bituthene 4000 combines a robust, flexible, pre-formed membrane made of high-performance cross-laminated HDPE carrier film with a tacky, self-adhesive rubberized asphalt compound, used with Bituthene 4000 surface conditioner (a water-based primer). It is a post-applied sheet waterproofing system suitable for below-grade vertical walls and has protected buildings worldwide for over 50 years, forming a complete system with Preprufe pre-applied membranes.
sow_steps: Prepare the substrate wall surface to be smooth, dry, and free of voids, fins, and sharp protrusions.
Apply Bituthene 4000 surface conditioner (primer) uniformly and allow to dry to a tack-free film.
Position and unroll Bituthene 4000 membrane sheets, removing the release liner as the sheet is pressed firmly into place.
Overlap adjacent sheets by the specified minimum width and roll firmly to ensure full adhesive contact.
Detail all corners, penetrations, and terminations with Bituthene Liquid Membrane for continuous protection.
Install a protection board (e.g., Hydroduct drainage composite) over the completed membrane before backfilling.
Backfill carefully in lifts to avoid damaging the membrane or protection board.

full_name: PREPRUFE® DLM / Bituthene® Liquid Membrane Two-Component Liquid Detailing Compound
description: Preprufe DLM (Bituthene Liquid Membrane) is a two-component, liquid-applied detailing compound providing good adhesion and high tensile strength for sealing complex details, penetrations, and terminations that cannot be effectively sealed with sheet membrane alone. It is low-VOC, isocyanate-free, and fully compatible with both the Preprufe and Bituthene sheet membrane systems, forming a complete detailing solution for UAE below-grade waterproofing projects.
sow_steps: Ensure the detail area (pipe penetration, corner, or termination) is clean, dry, and free of dust or debris.
Wrap the pipe or penetration point with Preprufe Tape as the first stage of detailing where applicable.
Mix the two components of Bituthene Liquid Membrane per manufacturer ratio using a low-speed drill.
Trowel or brush-apply the mixed compound around the penetration or detail as a fillet, ensuring full coverage over the Preprufe Tape edges.
Build up the fillet to the specified thickness, ensuring a smooth transition to the adjoining membrane.
Allow the compound to cure fully before backfilling or covering with concrete.
Inspect all detailed areas for continuity and adhesion prior to proceeding with adjacent membrane installation.

full_name: Xypex Concentrate Crystalline Waterproofing Coating for Concrete
description: Xypex Concentrate is the most chemically active product within the Xypex Crystalline Waterproofing System, consisting of Portland cement, finely graded sand, and active proprietary chemicals. When mixed with water it is applied as a cementitious slurry coat that generates a non-soluble crystalline formation throughout the pores and capillary tracts of concrete, permanently sealing against water penetration from any direction, even under high hydrostatic pressure; it is also used in Dry-Pac form for construction joints and crack/honeycomb repair. Xypex operates a dedicated UAE distributor network (da.xypex.com).
sow_steps: Prepare the concrete substrate by removing laitance, curing compounds, and loose material via mechanical abrasion or acid etching.
Saturate the substrate with clean water to achieve an SSD condition before application.
Mix Xypex Concentrate powder with clean water to a lump-free, brushable/sprayable slurry consistency.
Apply the first coat evenly by stiff brush or spray equipment across the entire surface.
Apply a second coat (if specified) once the first coat has set, typically the same or following day.
Use Xypex Concentrate in Dry-Pac form to seal construction joints, cracks, and honeycombed areas separately.
Moist-cure the treated surface for the recommended period to allow full crystalline growth before exposure to water.

full_name: Fosroc Renderoc HB
description: Renderoc HB is a lightweight, high-build, one-component, polymer and fibre-modified cementitious repair mortar meeting EN 1504-3 Class R2. It can be hand-applied or wet-sprayed in single layers from 10-80mm on vertical surfaces and up to 50mm overhead without the need for formwork, making it well suited to the Gulf's hot climate.
sow_steps: Step 1: Assess and sound out the concrete using hammer tap/chain drag survey to delineate the full extent of delamination and spalling
Step 2: Break out unsound/contaminated concrete to a minimum 15-20mm behind exposed reinforcement using mechanical breakers or hydro-demolition
Step 3: Clean and de-rust exposed rebar to Sa2.5/near-white metal and apply Fosroc Nitoprime Zincrich anti-corrosion coating
Step 4: Saturate the substrate with water to achieve a saturated surface dry (SSD) condition; apply Renderoc bonding slurry if specified
Step 5: Mix Renderoc HB with clean water per TDS ratio and apply by trowel or spray in single lifts up to 80mm vertical / 50mm overhead
Step 6: Finish to match surrounding profile using a wooden or sponge float while material is still workable
Step 7: Cure with wet hessian, curing membrane, or polythene sheeting for a minimum of 3-7 days per manufacturer guidance

full_name: Fosroc Renderoc HB40, HB40 Plus, HB70
description: Higher-strength variants of the Renderoc HB range (R3/R4 class per EN1504-3) formulated to match 25-50MPa parent concrete. HB70 is compatible with Fosroc Galvashield embedded galvanic anodes for combined structural repair and corrosion control on critical structures such as bridges and marine structures.
sow_steps: Step 1: Carry out condition survey and delamination mapping; test for chloride/carbonation ingress
Step 2: Remove defective concrete to sound substrate, undercutting reinforcement by 20mm minimum
Step 3: Clean rebar and install Galvashield anodes if specified for corrosion protection
Step 4: Prime rebar and substrate with recommended anti-corrosion/bonding primer
Step 5: Mix and apply Renderoc HB40/HB70 by trowel or spray in the specified maximum lift thickness
Step 6: Compact and finish surface to match adjacent profile
Step 7: Cure under damp hessian/curing compound for the manufacturer-specified duration

full_name: Fosroc Renderoc HS, HSXtra
description: Renderoc HS and HSXtra are shrinkage-compensated, polymer-modified, fibre-reinforced structural repair mortars that can be sprayed or trowelled in a single layer up to 50mm thick. They are widely specified in the UAE for structural concrete repair on bridges, parking structures and marine works where high early strength and low permeability are required.
sow_steps: Step 1: Survey and mark out deteriorated concrete extents via hammer sounding/half-cell potential survey
Step 2: Break out unsound concrete and clean exposed reinforcement to bright metal
Step 3: Apply anti-corrosion primer to reinforcement (e.g. Nitoprime Zincrich)
Step 4: Pre-wet substrate to SSD condition
Step 5: Spray or trowel apply Renderoc HS/HSXtra up to 50mm in a single pass
Step 6: Finish with float/trowel to match surrounding surface texture
Step 7: Apply curing compound or wet cure for a minimum of 3 days

full_name: Fosroc Renderoc TG
description: Renderoc TG is a general-purpose, medium-weight, polymer-modified repair mortar formulated for hot-climate application, offering good workability and finish for smaller-scale patch repairs on facades and general concrete elements.
sow_steps: Step 1: Identify and mark defective areas via visual inspection and sounding
Step 2: Cut back to sound concrete and remove all loose material and contamination
Step 3: Treat exposed reinforcement with anti-corrosion primer
Step 4: Dampen substrate to SSD condition
Step 5: Mix and hand-apply Renderoc TG by trowel to the required profile
Step 6: Finish surface to match adjacent texture
Step 7: Cure with wet hessian or curing membrane for 3+ days

full_name: Sika MonoTop-615 (HB)
description: Sika MonoTop-615 is a one-component, cement-based, polymer-modified, high-build structural repair mortar meeting EN1504-3 Class R3, achieving 35 N/mm2 compressive strength at 28 days. It is used for reprofiling and structural repair of reinforced concrete elements in a single high-build application.
sow_steps: Step 1: Survey substrate condition and identify defective concrete boundaries
Step 2: Remove unsound concrete mechanically to expose sound substrate and reinforcement
Step 3: Clean and treat reinforcement with Sika anti-corrosion primer (e.g. SikaTop Armatec-110 EpoCem)
Step 4: Saturate substrate to SSD condition prior to application
Step 5: Mix MonoTop-615 with clean water and apply by trowel or spray in high-build single layer
Step 6: Finish with a wet sponge or float to achieve required surface texture
Step 7: Cure using wet curing or Sika curing compound for minimum 3 days

full_name: Sika MonoTop-412 MY
description: Sika MonoTop-412 MY is a fibre-reinforced, low-shrinkage, one-component cementitious structural repair mortar for layer thicknesses of 6-50mm, mixed at approximately 3.5-4.0 litres of water per 25kg bag, suitable for the UAE's hot and humid conditions.
sow_steps: Step 1: Assess defect depth and extent via chain-drag/hammer sounding survey
Step 2: Remove deteriorated concrete to sound substrate and expose corroded rebar
Step 3: Clean rebar to bright metal and apply corrosion-inhibiting primer
Step 4: Pre-saturate substrate to SSD condition
Step 5: Mix MonoTop-412 MY to correct water ratio and apply by trowel in layers 6-50mm thick
Step 6: Finish surface with float to blend with surrounding concrete
Step 7: Cure with damp hessian or curing compound for at least 3 days

full_name: BASF MasterEmaco S 348
description: MasterEmaco S 348 is a dual shrinkage-compensated, trowelable, fibre-reinforced, thixotropic cementitious repair mortar applied in thicknesses of 12-50mm on vertical and overhead surfaces, providing high early strength and durability for structural concrete restoration.
sow_steps: Step 1: Conduct condition assessment and delamination survey of the structure
Step 2: Remove unsound/contaminated concrete to expose clean substrate and reinforcement
Step 3: Clean reinforcement and apply MasterProtect/MasterEmaco anti-corrosion primer
Step 4: Saturate substrate to SSD condition; apply bonding slurry if required
Step 5: Mix MasterEmaco S 348 and trowel-apply in lifts of 12-50mm
Step 6: Finish to match surrounding profile using float or trowel
Step 7: Cure with wet hessian, curing compound, or polythene sheeting per manufacturer TDS

full_name: BASF MasterEmaco S 5400
description: MasterEmaco S 5400 is a high-strength, shrinkage-compensated, fibre-reinforced structural repair mortar meeting EN1504-3 Class R4, applied in thicknesses of 5-50mm. It is specified for critical infrastructure such as bridge columns/piers, cooling towers, and marine structures requiring superior mechanical performance.
sow_steps: Step 1: Perform structural condition survey to identify repair extents
Step 2: Break out defective concrete to sound substrate with minimum 20mm rebar undercut
Step 3: Clean and prime reinforcement with anti-corrosion coating
Step 4: Saturate substrate to SSD condition
Step 5: Apply MasterEmaco S 5400 by trowel or spray in lifts of 5-50mm
Step 6: Finish surface to required texture and tolerance
Step 7: Cure per manufacturer instructions for minimum 3-7 days

full_name: Mapei Mapegrout T60 ME
description: Mapegrout T60 ME is a thixotropic, fibre-reinforced, sulphate and chloride-resistant structural repair mortar formulated specifically for the Middle East (EN1504-3 Class R4), applied in thicknesses of 10-60mm vertical / up to 30mm overhead, designed to withstand the region's aggressive ground and marine salt conditions.
sow_steps: Step 1: Survey structure for chloride/sulphate-induced deterioration and delamination
Step 2: Remove contaminated/unsound concrete to expose sound substrate and reinforcement
Step 3: Clean rebar and apply Mapefer or similar anti-corrosion coating
Step 4: Pre-wet substrate to SSD condition
Step 5: Mix Mapegrout T60 ME and trowel/spray apply in lifts up to 60mm vertical / 30mm overhead
Step 6: Finish surface to match existing profile
Step 7: Cure with wet hessian or Mapecure curing compound for minimum 3 days

full_name: Mapei Mapegrout T40
description: Mapegrout T40 is a medium-strength (40MPa), shrinkage-compensated structural repair mortar (EN1504-3 Class R3) suited to general reinforced concrete repairs where R4-level performance is not required.
sow_steps: Step 1: Assess and mark defective concrete zones
Step 2: Remove unsound concrete to sound substrate
Step 3: Clean and treat exposed reinforcement
Step 4: Saturate substrate to SSD condition
Step 5: Mix and apply Mapegrout T40 by trowel to required thickness
Step 6: Finish to match surrounding surface
Step 7: Cure per Mapei TDS recommendations, minimum 3 days

full_name: Euclid Chemical Euco Thixo RM
description: Euco Thixo RM is a shrinkage-compensated, fibre-reinforced, high-strength cementitious repair mortar composed of quality cement, graded aggregates, fibres and special additives, distributed in the UAE via StonCor Middle East/Tremco Middle East for structural concrete repair.
sow_steps: Step 1: Survey and delineate defective concrete extents
Step 2: Remove unsound concrete to sound substrate, exposing corroded reinforcement
Step 3: Clean and prime reinforcement with anti-corrosion coating
Step 4: Saturate substrate to SSD condition
Step 5: Mix and trowel-apply Euco Thixo RM to required thickness
Step 6: Finish to match surrounding profile
Step 7: Cure with wet curing or curing compound per TDS

full_name: Euclid Chemical TAMMS Structural Mortar
description: TAMMS Structural Mortar is a single-component repair mortar applied by low-pressure spray or by hand for structural concrete repairs, offering fibre reinforcement for crack resistance and long-term durability.
sow_steps: Step 1: Inspect and mark repair boundaries via sounding survey
Step 2: Remove deteriorated concrete to sound substrate
Step 3: Clean and treat exposed reinforcement
Step 4: Saturate substrate to SSD condition
Step 5: Spray or hand-apply TAMMS Structural Mortar to required thickness
Step 6: Finish surface to match adjacent concrete
Step 7: Moist cure or apply curing compound for minimum 3 days

full_name: Sika Sikadur-52 LP AE
description: Sikadur-52 LP AE is a two-part, solvent-free, low-viscosity epoxy resin engineered for hot/tropical climates, used to inject and structurally bond cracks 0.2-5mm wide in concrete, achieving compressive strength ≥70 N/mm2 at 7 days/30°C. It is a UAE-specific formulation available from Sika UAE LLC.
sow_steps: Step 1: Inspect and map cracks; determine if dormant or active/moving and measure crack width
Step 2: Clean crack faces of dust, laitance, and contamination using compressed air/vacuum
Step 3: Install surface injection ports at intervals along the crack line and seal the crack surface between ports with epoxy paste
Step 4: Mix Sikadur-52 LP AE components A and B per specified ratio using a low-speed drill
Step 5: Inject resin under low pressure sequentially from lowest port until resin emerges at adjacent port, then seal and move to next port
Step 6: Allow resin to cure per TDS schedule (typically 24-48 hours depending on temperature)
Step 7: Remove surface seal and ports, grind flush, and finish surface as required

full_name: Sika Sikadur-52 LP
description: Sikadur-52 LP is the standard-climate variant of Sika's low-viscosity epoxy crack injection resin, used for structural bonding and sealing of cracks in concrete elements via pressure injection.
sow_steps: Step 1: Survey and classify cracks by width and activity
Step 2: Clean crack surfaces and drill injection ports along the crack
Step 3: Seal crack face with epoxy paste, leaving ports exposed
Step 4: Mix Sikadur-52 LP components per ratio specified
Step 5: Inject resin sequentially from the lowest point upward until full penetration achieved
Step 6: Allow curing per manufacturer schedule
Step 7: Remove ports and grind surface flush, finish as required

full_name: Fosroc Nitofill LV
description: Nitofill LV is a low-viscosity epoxy injection system for structural crack repair, capable of injecting cracks from 0.3mm to 9mm wide, provided both faces of the crack are accessible for sealing.
sow_steps: Step 1: Identify crack pattern, width, and depth via visual/ultrasonic survey
Step 2: Clean crack faces of debris and laitance
Step 3: Seal both faces of crack and install injection nipples/ports
Step 4: Mix Nitofill LV base and hardener per ratio
Step 5: Inject resin under controlled pressure from base upward until refusal
Step 6: Cure per manufacturer's schedule
Step 7: Remove ports, grind flush, and reinstate surface finish

full_name: Fosroc Nitofill EPLV
description: Nitofill EPLV is a two-part liquid epoxy injection resin for cracks 0.25-9mm wide, delivering compressive strength >60 N/mm2 at 7 days, tensile strength >15 N/mm2 and flexural strength >50 N/mm2, restoring structural monolithic behaviour to cracked concrete.
sow_steps: Step 1: Map crack widths and confirm suitability for epoxy injection (dormant cracks only)
Step 2: Clean cracks of dust and loose material
Step 3: Install injection ports and seal crack surface with epoxy paste
Step 4: Mix Nitofill EPLV components per manufacturer ratio
Step 5: Inject under pressure sequentially until resin exudes at next port
Step 6: Allow full cure per TDS schedule
Step 7: Remove seal/ports and finish surface

full_name: Fosroc Nitofill WS60, UR63, PU150
description: Polyurethane-based injection resin variants within the Nitofill range designed for sealing live, water-bearing cracks and joints where water ingress must be stopped rather than structural bonding restored.
sow_steps: Step 1: Locate active water leaks and map crack/joint routes
Step 2: Clean and prepare crack surface, install injection packers
Step 3: Mix PU resin components per specified ratio
Step 4: Inject PU resin under pressure to react with water and form flexible foam/gel seal
Step 5: Allow reaction and expansion to stop water flow
Step 6: Remove packers and patch injection points
Step 7: Monitor for re-emergence of leaks and re-inject if required

full_name: Hilti HIT-RE 500 V4
description: HIT-RE 500 V4 is an injectable epoxy mortar for rebar dowelling and anchor connections in cracked and uncracked concrete (C12/15 to C50/60), usable in dry, wet, submerged, and water-filled holes, with ETA seismic C1/C2 approval for structural anchoring applications.
sow_steps: Step 1: Determine anchor/rebar layout and embedment depth per design (Hilti PROFIS software)
Step 2: Drill hole to specified diameter and depth using appropriate drill bit
Step 3: Clean hole using Hilti hollow drill bit system or manual brush/blow-out procedure per ETA requirements
Step 4: Dispense HIT-RE 500 V4 through static mixer, discarding initial non-mixed material
Step 5: Fill hole from bottom to approximately two-thirds full, avoiding air entrapment
Step 6: Insert rebar/anchor with slight rotation to distribute resin, brace if required
Step 7: Allow full cure time per temperature-dependent schedule before loading

full_name: Euclid Chemical Dural 618LV
description: Dural 618LV is a two-component, solvent-free structural epoxy liquid injection adhesive designed for deep penetration into hairline cracks, sealing and restoring the structural integrity of concrete, available through Euclid's UAE distribution network.
sow_steps: Step 1: Survey and classify crack widths and depths
Step 2: Clean crack faces of dust and debris
Step 3: Seal crack surface and install injection ports
Step 4: Mix Dural 618LV components per specified ratio
Step 5: Inject resin under low pressure from lowest port upward
Step 6: Cure per manufacturer's schedule
Step 7: Remove ports, grind flush, and finish surface

full_name: Euclid Chemical Dural 619PU, Dural 620PU
description: Dural 619PU is a two-component, low-viscosity, solvent-free water-reactive fast-foaming polyurethane injection resin; Dural 620PU is a low-viscosity PU injection resin for grouting and sealing water-bearing cracks and fissures in concrete and masonry.
sow_steps: Step 1: Identify active leak paths and crack locations
Step 2: Clean crack surface and install injection packers
Step 3: Mix PU resin components per ratio
Step 4: Inject resin under pressure to react with water ingress and expand/foam to seal
Step 5: Allow reaction time for full expansion and cure
Step 6: Remove packers and patch entry points
Step 7: Verify leak stoppage and re-inject if necessary

full_name: Sika CarboDur S, M, UH
description: Sika CarboDur is a range of pultruded carbon fibre reinforced polymer (CFRP) laminates with fibre volumetric content greater than 68%, bonded to concrete surfaces using Sikadur-30 structural epoxy adhesive. Tensile strength ranges from 2.8-3.1 GPa (Type S) with E-modulus from 165-400 GPa depending on type, used to increase flexural and shear capacity of existing structures.
sow_steps: Step 1: Conduct structural assessment and design CFRP strengthening scheme using Sika design software/engineering support
Step 2: Prepare concrete substrate by grinding/grit-blasting to remove laitance and achieve required surface profile and tensile pull-off strength
Step 3: Clean substrate of dust and ensure it is dry
Step 4: Cut CarboDur laminate to required length and clean with solvent wipe
Step 5: Mix Sikadur-30 epoxy adhesive and apply to both the substrate and laminate in a rounded profile
Step 6: Apply laminate to substrate and press firmly, rolling out excess adhesive and air voids from center outward
Step 7: Cure per epoxy adhesive schedule; protect from moisture/UV until full cure, then apply protective coating if specified

full_name: Fosroc Nitowrap FRC, CW, CF, XS
description: Nitowrap is Fosroc's carbon fibre sheet (fabric) strengthening system, comprising unidirectional carbon fibre fabric applied wet-laid with Nitowrap primer, saturant, and protective topcoat, used to enhance flexural, shear and confinement capacity of concrete columns, beams, and slabs.
sow_steps: Step 1: Carry out structural assessment and design strengthening layout (fibre orientation and number of layers)
Step 2: Prepare substrate by grinding to remove laitance and rounding sharp corners (for column wrapping)
Step 3: Clean surface of dust and apply Nitowrap primer to substrate
Step 4: Apply levelling putty to fill surface voids if required
Step 5: Apply saturant resin, lay carbon fibre fabric, and impregnate with roller to remove air voids
Step 6: Apply additional saturant coat and subsequent layers as per design, ensuring proper overlap
Step 7: Apply protective topcoat once cured and allow full cure prior to service loading

full_name: Hilti HIT-Shear and HIT-Punching Shear Strengthening System
description: Hilti's punching shear and shear strengthening toolkit combines post-installed shear reinforcement bars with HIT-RE 500 injection adhesive to retrofit existing flat slabs and beams for increased shear/punching shear capacity without full-depth demolition.
sow_steps: Step 1: Conduct structural assessment to determine required additional shear capacity
Step 2: Design reinforcement layout using Hilti PROFIS engineering software
Step 3: Drill holes at required angle and depth through slab/beam
Step 4: Clean holes per ETA-approved cleaning procedure
Step 5: Inject HIT-RE 500 adhesive and insert shear reinforcement bars/studs
Step 6: Allow curing per temperature-dependent schedule
Step 7: Verify installation via proof testing where specified

full_name: Sika SikaCem Gunit-133, Sikacrete-503, Sikacrete Gunite-105 FP
description: Sika's shotcrete/gunite range covers dry-mix and wet-mix sprayable cementitious repair mortars used for large-area structural repair and new concrete overlay applications where formwork is impractical, such as tunnel linings, retaining walls, and large-surface repairs.
sow_steps: Step 1: Assess substrate condition and design shotcrete repair thickness and reinforcement mesh if required
Step 2: Remove unsound concrete and prepare substrate to required profile
Step 3: Clean and prime reinforcement with corrosion inhibitor
Step 4: Install reinforcing mesh/anchors if specified by design
Step 5: Pre-wet substrate and spray-apply shotcrete mix in successive passes to build up required thickness
Step 6: Finish surface using rod and float or leave as-sprayed per specification
Step 7: Cure with wet curing or curing compound for the specified duration to control plastic shrinkage

product_name: Renderoc HS (Sprayable) / Guncrete E / Renderoc CAC Dry Spray
full_name: Fosroc Renderoc HS Sprayable, Guncrete E, Renderoc CAC Dry Spray
description: Fosroc's sprayable shotcrete/gunite repair products, including Renderoc HS in sprayable form and Guncrete E, are used for rapid large-area concrete repair and reprofiling on vertical and overhead surfaces without formwork, common in UAE infrastructure and tunnel projects.
sow_steps: Step 1: Survey structure and determine repair thickness/reinforcement requirements
Step 2: Remove deteriorated concrete and prepare substrate profile
Step 3: Treat exposed reinforcement with anti-corrosion primer
Step 4: Fix reinforcing mesh where required by design
Step 5: Pre-wet substrate and wet/dry-spray apply mortar in successive layers to build thickness
Step 6: Finish surface per specification (rod, float, or sprayed finish)
Step 7: Cure using wet hessian or curing membrane per manufacturer schedule

full_name: BASF MasterEmaco S 820, S 880
description: MasterEmaco S 820 and S 880 are dry-mix shotcrete repair mortars designed for large-scale structural repair and encasement applications, offering low rebound, high early strength, and durability for sprayed concrete repair work.
sow_steps: Step 1: Assess substrate and design shotcrete application thickness
Step 2: Remove unsound concrete to expose sound substrate
Step 3: Clean and prime reinforcement
Step 4: Pre-wet substrate to SSD condition
Step 5: Dry/wet spray apply MasterEmaco S 820/S 880 in successive passes
Step 6: Finish surface per specification
Step 7: Cure with wet curing or curing compound for specified duration

full_name: Euclid Chemical Eucoshot
description: Eucoshot is a silica-fume modified, dry shotcrete repair material available in fibre-reinforced, water-repellent, and standard formulations, used for large-area sprayed concrete repair.
sow_steps: Step 1: Assess substrate and design shotcrete thickness/reinforcement
Step 2: Remove deteriorated concrete to sound substrate
Step 3: Clean and prime exposed reinforcement
Step 4: Pre-wet substrate
Step 5: Dry-spray apply Eucoshot in successive layers
Step 6: Finish surface per specification
Step 7: Cure per manufacturer's recommended schedule

full_name: Sika FerroGard-903, FerroGard-903+
description: Sika FerroGard-903 is a penetrating, amino-alcohol based corrosion inhibitor applied to the surface of existing reinforced concrete to migrate through the concrete matrix and protect embedded reinforcement, extending service life without the need for concrete removal.
sow_steps: Step 1: Assess chloride/carbonation levels via core sampling and testing
Step 2: Clean concrete surface of dirt, coatings, and contamination
Step 3: Ensure substrate is sound and free of active leaks
Step 4: Apply FerroGard-903 by brush, roller, or spray in the specified number of coats
Step 5: Allow product to fully penetrate and dry between coats per TDS
Step 6: Apply protective/decorative coating over treated surface if specified
Step 7: Monitor corrosion rate periodically via half-cell potential testing

full_name: Fosroc Nitoprime Zincrich, Nitoprime Zincrich Plus
description: Nitoprime Zincrich is a two-part, zinc-rich epoxy primer applied to cleaned reinforcement prior to patch repair, providing cathodic/galvanic protection to steel and reducing the risk of incipient anode corrosion at repair interfaces.
sow_steps: Step 1: Expose and clean reinforcement to Sa2.5/bright metal standard by grit-blasting or needle-gunning
Step 2: Ensure steel is free of oil, grease, and loose rust
Step 3: Mix Nitoprime Zincrich base and hardener per ratio
Step 4: Apply by brush in a continuous coat fully encapsulating exposed rebar circumference
Step 5: Allow to touch-dry before proceeding to repair mortar application
Step 6: Apply Renderoc bonding slurry/repair mortar within recoat window specified
Step 7: Inspect coverage prior to closing up repair

full_name: Fosroc Nitoprime 120
description: Nitoprime 120 is a general-purpose bonding/anti-corrosion primer used to enhance adhesion between repair mortars and prepared reinforcement or substrate.
sow_steps: Step 1: Clean reinforcement/substrate to remove rust, oil, and contamination
Step 2: Mix Nitoprime 120 per manufacturer instructions
Step 3: Apply by brush in a uniform coat
Step 4: Allow to become tacky/touch-dry per TDS window
Step 5: Apply repair mortar while primer is within recoat window
Step 6: Verify full coverage before closing repair
Step 7: Proceed to structural repair mortar application

full_name: BASF MasterProtect 8000CI, 8500CI, 8020CI
description: The MasterProtect CI range comprises silane/siloxane-based penetrating corrosion inhibitors that migrate through concrete cover to form a protective barrier around embedded reinforcement, reducing chloride-induced corrosion risk in existing structures.
sow_steps: Step 1: Test concrete for chloride content and carbonation depth
Step 2: Clean concrete surface of coatings, dirt, and laitance
Step 3: Ensure surface is dry and free of standing water
Step 4: Apply MasterProtect CI product by low-pressure spray in the specified dosage/coats
Step 5: Allow full penetration and drying time between coats
Step 6: Apply topcoat/protective coating if specified in design
Step 7: Periodically monitor corrosion activity via electrochemical testing

full_name: Euclid Chemical EUCON ACI 2000M
description: EUCON ACI 2000M is an organic corrosion inhibitor admixture complying with ASTM G109, dosed into fresh concrete or repair mortar to reduce the rate of chloride-induced reinforcement corrosion, available through Euclid's UAE distribution network.
sow_steps: Step 1: Determine required dosage based on exposure conditions and chloride risk
Step 2: Add EUCON ACI 2000M to concrete/mortar mix during batching
Step 3: Mix thoroughly to ensure uniform dispersion
Step 4: Place and compact concrete/mortar per standard procedures
Step 5: Finish surface as required
Step 6: Cure per standard concrete curing practice
Step 7: Monitor long-term corrosion performance where specified

full_name: Fosroc Conbextra GP
description: Conbextra GP is a general-purpose, non-shrink cementitious grout supplied as a ready-to-use dry powder that, when mixed with water, produces a flowing grout for gap thicknesses up to 100mm. It meets ASTM C1107 with 28-day compressive strength of 66 N/mm2, and is used to fill voids beneath base plates, machinery, and for anchoring bolts and fixings.
sow_steps: Step 1: Assess gap thickness and design formwork/shuttering for grout containment
Step 2: Clean substrate and base plate of oil, laitance, and debris; saturate substrate with water
Step 3: Remove standing water and construct grout head/pouring box
Step 4: Mix Conbextra GP with clean water per water:powder ratio (typically 0.18) using a mechanical mixer
Step 5: Pour grout continuously from one side to avoid air entrapment until it flows out the opposite side
Step 6: Remove formwork after initial set and trim excess grout
Step 7: Cure exposed grout edges with wet hessian or curing compound for minimum 3 days

full_name: Sika SikaGrout-214, SikaGrout-214 AE, SikaGrout-214 TR
description: SikaGrout-214 is a non-shrink, high-strength, flowable precision cementitious grout used for grouting base plates, machine foundations, and structural connections, with AE and TR variants formulated for hot climates and extended flow time respectively.
sow_steps: Step 1: Assess gap dimensions and prepare formwork/shuttering
Step 2: Clean and saturate substrate and base plate surfaces
Step 3: Remove standing water before pouring
Step 4: Mix SikaGrout-214 with clean water to correct consistency using mechanical mixer
Step 5: Pour continuously from one side, ensuring grout flows fully through the gap without air voids
Step 6: Strike off excess grout and remove formwork after initial set
Step 7: Cure exposed edges with wet hessian or curing compound

full_name: BASF MasterFlow 928, MasterFlow 928T
description: MasterFlow 928 is a high-strength, non-shrink cementitious grout used for precision grouting of machine bases, structural bearings, and column base plates, with the 928T variant offering extended working/flow time for hot-climate placement.
sow_steps: Step 1: Assess grouting gap and design formwork
Step 2: Clean and saturate substrate; remove standing water
Step 3: Mix MasterFlow 928 with clean water per specified ratio
Step 4: Pour grout continuously from one side to avoid trapping air
Step 5: Ensure full contact under base plate using appropriate pouring technique
Step 6: Remove formwork after initial set and trim excess material
Step 7: Cure exposed grout surfaces per manufacturer schedule

full_name: Euclid Chemical Euco Grout GP, Euco 530 NS
description: Euco Grout GP is a premixed cementitious non-shrink grout that produces a free-flowing grout of excellent workability when mixed with water; Euco 530 NS is a cement-based non-shrink grout for general construction grouting applications, both distributed in the UAE through StonCor/Tremco Middle East.
sow_steps: Step 1: Assess gap dimensions and prepare formwork
Step 2: Clean and saturate substrate; remove standing water
Step 3: Mix grout with clean water to specified consistency
Step 4: Pour continuously from one side to prevent air entrapment
Step 5: Ensure full contact and flow beneath base plate
Step 6: Remove formwork after initial set
Step 7: Cure exposed grout with wet hessian or curing compound

full_name: Euclid Chemical E3-Flowable, E3-HCR Epoxy Grout
description: E3-Flowable is a 3-component, high-flow, high-strength expansive epoxy grout for large plates and narrow configurations requiring high flowability; E3-HCR is a highly chemical-resistant novolac epoxy grout designed for industrial applications exposed to concentrated acids, alkalis, and solvents.
sow_steps: Step 1: Assess grouting gap and chemical exposure conditions
Step 2: Prepare formwork and clean substrate/base plate thoroughly, ensuring dry surface
Step 3: Mix the three epoxy grout components per manufacturer ratio
Step 4: Pour epoxy grout continuously from one side to avoid air entrapment
Step 5: Ensure full contact beneath base plate
Step 6: Allow to cure per epoxy schedule before loading
Step 7: Trim and finish exposed edges

full_name: Sika Sikagard-675 W GB ElastoColor
description: Sikagard-675 W GB ElastoColor is an elastomeric, anti-carbonation protective coating meeting EN 1504-2, designed to bridge cracks and prevent CO2/chloride ingress into concrete while providing a decorative, weather-resistant finish for exposed structures.
sow_steps: Step 1: Assess substrate condition and confirm compatibility with coating system
Step 2: Clean and repair surface defects, cracks, and spalls prior to coating
Step 3: Apply primer coat where specified for substrate porosity
Step 4: Apply Sikagard-675 W GB ElastoColor by roller, brush, or airless spray in first coat
Step 5: Allow to dry per TDS recoat window
Step 6: Apply second coat to achieve specified dry film thickness
Step 7: Allow full cure before exposure to weather/traffic per manufacturer schedule

full_name: Sika Sikagard PW
description: Sikagard PW is a chemical-resistant epoxy protective coating for concrete surfaces, holding Dubai Green Building certification, used in industrial and potable water-related applications requiring durable chemical protection.
sow_steps: Step 1: Assess substrate and confirm suitability for epoxy coating application
Step 2: Prepare surface by grinding/shot-blasting to remove laitance
Step 3: Clean and dry substrate thoroughly
Step 4: Apply primer coat if specified
Step 5: Mix and apply Sikagard PW epoxy coating by roller or spray
Step 6: Apply additional coats as required for specified DFT
Step 7: Allow full cure before service exposure

product_name: Dekguard WB300 / S / PU / AC / E2000
full_name: Fosroc Dekguard WB300, Dekguard S, Dekguard PU, Dekguard AC, Dekguard E2000
description: The Dekguard range comprises anti-carbonation, waterproof protective coatings formulated to protect reinforced concrete structures from CO2, chloride, and water ingress, available in acrylic (WB300, S, AC), polyurethane (PU), and epoxy (E2000) chemistries for varying exposure and aesthetic requirements.
sow_steps: Step 1: Assess substrate condition and select appropriate Dekguard variant for exposure conditions
Step 2: Repair surface defects and cracks prior to coating application
Step 3: Apply Dekguard Primer WB or specified primer to substrate
Step 4: Apply first coat of Dekguard by roller, brush, or spray
Step 5: Allow to dry per recoat window specified in TDS
Step 6: Apply second coat to achieve required dry film thickness
Step 7: Allow full cure prior to weather/service exposure

full_name: BASF MasterProtect 300, 300FC, 150, 1812, 1815, 330 EL
description: The MasterProtect coating range includes anti-carbonation, crack-bridging, and elastomeric protective coatings for concrete facades and structures, providing long-term protection against carbonation, chloride ingress, and weathering while offering decorative finish options.
sow_steps: Step 1: Assess substrate condition and confirm coating compatibility
Step 2: Repair cracks and surface defects prior to coating
Step 3: Apply primer per substrate porosity and product requirements
Step 4: Apply first coat of MasterProtect coating by roller/spray
Step 5: Allow to dry per recoat window
Step 6: Apply second coat to achieve specified DFT
Step 7: Allow full cure before weather exposure

full_name: Euclid Chemical Tammoseal Flex, Tammscoat AC
description: Tammoseal Flex is a two-component, cement-based, polymer-modified elastomeric coating with crack-bridging ability for waterproofing concrete and masonry; Tammscoat AC is an elastomeric, water-based acrylic anti-carbonation coating offering elongation, recovery, and UV/rain resistance.
sow_steps: Step 1: Assess substrate condition and confirm coating suitability
Step 2: Repair cracks and defects prior to coating
Step 3: Clean and prime substrate as specified
Step 4: Apply first coat by brush, roller, or spray
Step 5: Allow to dry per recoat window
Step 6: Apply subsequent coats to achieve specified thickness
Step 7: Cure per manufacturer schedule before service exposure

full_name: Mapei Elastocolor Protective Coating Range
description: Mapei's Elastocolor and related protective coating range provide elastomeric, crack-bridging, anti-carbonation protection for concrete facades and structures in the UAE, complementing the Mapegrout repair mortar system for a complete repair-and-protect solution.
sow_steps: Step 1: Assess substrate condition and confirm suitability of coating system
Step 2: Repair surface defects using compatible Mapegrout repair mortar
Step 3: Prime substrate as specified
Step 4: Apply first coat of Elastocolor by roller/spray
Step 5: Allow to dry per recoat window
Step 6: Apply second coat to achieve required film thickness
Step 7: Allow full cure before service/weather exposure
